Size down, comfortable and great value I read tons of reviews but still wasn’t sure on a S or M. I got both for sizing and will be returning the M which I didn’t even try on because the small fit perfectly. I’m 5’2”, 135 lbs pre pregnancy, flabbier build and no booty. It’s true that these run big because I would not normally order a S in anything for my shape. I’m currently 28 weeks and 150 lbs with more growing to do. These fit great right now but I could stand to fill the belly band more which means they will probably get me through the summer all the way to delivery (due early August). I thought they would have more stretch but I’m not complaining. They’re comfortable and cute and really the perfect length. I do wish the front pockets were functional!! Will be ordering the other color in a S. Can’t beat the price.

Great jean short for the price! I've had a reallyyyyyy hard time finding decent maternity clothing in general, and I'm pretty picky about my jean shorts. I'm the typical American Eagle jean short wearer, so I was hoping these would be stretchy like those, and they sure are!! My ONLY complaint is they don't fit close enough to the bum around the legs (I hate the diaper butt look), but I could've sized down and that would probably be fixed. They are very comfortable and have ample amount of stretch. The length is great too — short enough to be "hip" but not too short to be hoochie mama : ) For reference I'm 5'3, 145lbs, 6 months pregnant, and wore a size small before getting pregnant in all bottoms (was 130lbs). I ordered these in a medium but probably could've ordered a small to fix the fit in the rear. They should consider making a "distressed" version of these!!

Uncomfortable Seem I'm 33 weeks along, 238 pounds, and stand at 5'6. All around these shorts felt nice in quality. The belly has a more loose than spandex material so I'm not sure how long that will hold up around the belly. But, the shorts are nice and snug though verging too tight. And I'm not sure yet if it's because of the size or the fact that the line in the picture I circled literally cuts into your skin. Out of all the stretch these shorts offer, that seem feels like a size too small with absolutely no stretch and it's these shorts biggest downfall. Its an unavoidable issue. Because while you may have had wiggle room to grow, that seem will stop you from fitting in them. I read in another review about it and still thought it was worth the shot considering maternity shorts, in my size, are hard to find. And while I'm almost happy with these. I dont know how long I'll be able to wear them if I gain much more, all because of that seem.

My only complaint is the pre-sewn ‘cuff’ along the bottom doesn’t stay rolled on the back so I’m constantly adjusting them. But the quality is great so overall I’m happy!

I’m very happy with these shorts! I bought both the light and dark colours. The dark is more blended than it looks in the pictures it’s not such a harshly obvious wave. They are a really perfect length not overly short but not frumpy long either. The Jean material is comfy and stretchy sort of like a jegging but with more structure than cheap jegging shorts so they look like normal Jean shorts. The panel that goes over your belly is made out of a fairly thin material compared to other maternity pants so they seem well suited to summer weather. Not so thin that it seems like it’ll rip but you can see light through the material when you hold it up to a window so I think it’ll be pretty breathable on a hotter day. The only thing to note is that the top of the panel goes up a little higher than my other maternity pants so it might not work for a very short woman but I’m 5”6 with a short torso and the top of it hits my bra but the material is thin enough that it’s not annoying

OK so I am 5' 11" and about 260lbs..measurements 46' 41' 55 hips..and I have been wanting a Vintage denim jacket that actually fits good since forever.. I looked at this for like a week..it's been in the cart but kept ignoring cause the 'XXL sizing was worrying me because a XXL and a 2xl are different sizes a lot of people don't realize.. so I was skeptical..omg it arrived way before it's arrival time of 10 pm next day..got it today and opened the bag.. when I pulled this jacket out.. it smelled vintage lol.. like old denim..that's a plus cause then I know it's not chambray it's real jeans material with no stretch.. and then I opened up the jacket and it was big big.. Then i gently closed my eyes while standing in front the mirror then put it on.. my big long arms slid in with so much ease.. and then the jacket actually met in front a d can be buttoned to my 😮 and surprise.. which is amazing for me. I am never this lucky. I live in nyc and we go through like 6 seasons in one fall day so some jackets are too hot. Plus I have a bunch of patches that's begging for a new home on a piece of clothing..the price at $43 bucks was on point and actually the best 43 bucks I spent in a while. I..waiting on my new pair of Doc Martens on Saturday and I am fall ready. THANK YOU TO THIS COMPANY. THIS JACKET IS PERFECT. A GOOD WASH WITH SOME SOFTENER WILL RID THE NOT SO TERRIBLE SMELL. ..edit...AND IT HAS THE TWO INSIDE POCKETS LIKE OLD JEANS JACKETS DO. 🥰

Close, No Cigar/Odd Construction Okay so my view of these and how I feel about them. Pairs bought:size 14 black, size 15 black.Material Contents: 98%cotton 2%Spandex each pair. I'm 5'6" 185-190lb on my heavy side rn, w/meas. of 38",33",46". Size 13-14 is my usual size currently. These in sz.14 are what I have on in all 4 pics.The waist fits me alright, the shorts as a whole feel like they suck me up with moderate belly/flab control, and is slightly forgiving with a small gap in the back as pictured. Although they're 98%cotton there is almost no breathability and very little stretch in the fabric and waist. The crotch is on the tighter side for me even though they have a longer 7" inseam, the cut is weird through the groin area and I wouldn't want to wear these in a hot humidity environment. Similar to how others have stated, I can see how these won't hold their shape after wear, they wrinkle easily and would have to be ironed even after the dryer, they flare/bow out at the bottom sides, and they are soft in the inside and outside however any kind of marks show up on this type of material especially since it's a darker shade of black. The front pockets are of decent sizing, can be difficult ti access with the shorts tighter around the hip and waist, and the back pockets exist except they're sewn in of which can be carefully cut out and removed. They are naturally snug around my problem area where my belly pooch is however this isn't the case with my other Lee shorts sz14. These are a nice casual short that I'd wear to a business-casual event outside or preferably inside where extrenuous movement wouldn't be involved although these would be my last choice if nothing else was available--this type of short is either for a unique body type, someone who doesn't mind the flaws, or the flaws work for them. Returning and on the hunt again.Happy Shopping y'all 😀

It works! I am 5’5 and around 165 lbs. I purchased this because I love jumpsuits but didn’t expect much. Well, I love the way it looks! Unfortunately, I sized up as per the reviews saying that it had no give and needed a size bigger if I had curves. It was not the case for me. The waist elastic was a bit loose and there was some gaping around the hips as you can see in the picture, so I sent it back and purchased a size M (pictures are of a size L). The fabric is really nice, thicker denim and looks like it will stretch out with time so I think the smaller size will be more flattering. It had a bit of a scent on arrival so you will need to wash it before wearing it. The pants touched the top of my feet (a bit longer than ankle length). It has buttons and not snaps. The top is a bit bigger than the bottom (another reason why I decided to go down a size). But the construction is very nice. If you like jumpsuits and the 90s look, this is for you! Update: I ordered the M Size and it is a bit tight. Such a bummer! Since I’m between sizes, I’m keeping the smaller one and hoping it will feel better once the extra Christmas weight goes away 🫣

Good quality, fits well... I ordered the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL. To start, these are not high waisted pants. They are hip huggers, meaning they sit low on your waist and will not restrict your tummy if you have a few extra pounds. Furthermore, they state they jeans are stretch. They have a slight stretch to them, but they are not what is normally considered a stretch pant. Finally, these are not baggy on the average girl. They are boot cut/flare leg, but not baggy.With all of that said, I am 5 foot, 5 inches tall, 275 pounds, wear an 18/20 USA size and a 2x/3x top. My waist measurement at belly button level is 46 inches. My upper thighs and calves are thicker than most. I was concerned these pants would not fit, however, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL fit comfortably. I will say there is a bit of tightness in the knee area, but I tend to have this issue due to my thick legs. I am not use to a hip hugger pant, so was genuinely surprised that these pants fit tight, but comfortably, as they sit under my "jelly roll", so there is none of that constrictive feeling on your stomach. I also like my pants longer and I found these to fit perfectly, touching the ground, giving them room to shrink a bit.Overall, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown in Size XXL are well made, the denim is thicker than most and I absolutely love the color. The fit is surprisingly good and the hip hugger style makes them extremely comfortable. So if you are a curvy girl like me, check these out. Five stars.

Cute distressed shorts My teen daughter weighs 115 lbs and she’s almost 5’6” tall. She wears shorts in size small or 4. I ordered these shorts in color 21148 size small for her and they are a perfect fit. The fabric content is 85% cotton 15% polyester. The shorts look exactly as they do in the pictures in the listing. There’s a lot of distressing and rips on the front. Underneath most of those rips there are white interior pockets that show through the rips where the shorts are being worn. On the back the rips are only on the back pockets. As you can see on my picture there was some uneven fringe distressing done on the back of the legs openings. My daughter trimmed the too long strings with scissors after taking the picture. The shorts are more skin revealing on the front but they have a full coverage on the back. They are high waisted. The stitching is ok overall but there was one spot in the front with missing stitch, and a few others will loose threads. I’m taking a star off for those sewing imperfections but my daughter really likes the fit and the look of these denim shorts and she wears them often.

Fantastic for what they are OK so first things first, I feel like some reviews need a but of a reminder that these are inexpensive Amazon shorts. They arent BAD quality, just don't be expecting the nicest of zippers or buttons. I have huge thighs compared to by waist (I wish that looked as good as it sounds but I promise it does not). My thighs will pull up any shorts at all unless I go full out knee length which I am not doing. Finding shorts to fit my thighs is near impossible. This will be my third pair. The stretch and little side slit helps immensely. For size, I got a medium. I'm about 175 5'6" 28" waist 43" hips. I can't see the size chart right now but I think that put me at an xl. After reading the reviews I kept seeing "i sized down and would have been fine doing so again". And if you have big thighs you know you have to err on the side of a little tight or you get that excess bulge of fabric in the middle that's not a good look. These fit super comfortably. As you can see, more on the snug side but not tight. They have plenty of give. If you don't have the spare fabric when you sit issue you can size down just one I think. Kinda wish there were options without lots of fringe that didn't involve cuffs (those REALLY don't work with thighs), because I'm not 13 anymore, but honestly I'm gonna trim it off, replace the buttons, and these will be worn a lot. Overall 10/10, I'm happy.

Stylish/Perfect/Comfortable!! These shorts are perfect!! I bought them to wear over my swimsuit, and they are so so so cute and stylish, but also modest... so I dont have half my buttcheeks hanging out. As a mommy of two very young kiddos, I hold my dress standards to a higher level, and no longer feel comfortable showing too much skin (totally my personal preference, so if you like your butt hanging out of your shorts.. then you go girl.. I just dont). So I really love these shorts for this reason, but on top of that.. they're also sooo dang cute and incredibly comfortable. I will say, as far as how they fit... that they are really big on me.. like I can pull them out and have over 6 inches of gap between my lower back and the back of the shorts. But I honestly dont care.. because they still feel great on and look cute. I think they're actually true to size, and I dont believe they run big at all... I just run small. Haha I'm only 5'3" and barely 100lbs... and I have to shop in the kids' section, so I usually expect adult clothing to be much too big for me. With that being said, these shorts are really amazing. You get the cute look of a cut off, without your entire butt hanging out. They're the perfect length, and I highly recommend them!!

Don't size up! I like the shorts, my problem is that I sized up after reading reviews. I am 5'3, 165lbs... heavy on the booty and thick thighs. I like my shorts bigger, but these might be a little too big. I ordered an XL, and I have no doubt that a L or maybe even M would fit fine. The legs have slits in the sides, which help them to not fit so tight. The waist seems really big, too. They are super stretchy. I'm not sure I'll keep these, but I might exchange them for a smaller size. Update: ordered the Large, in a different wash, and they were not nearly as stretchy as the other ones from before. They unfortunately did not fit as well as I prefer. I think the sizing truly depends on the wash you order.

Awesome Flattering Short Shorts. I always thought this design kinda looked like a waitress’s apron. But, luckily, in person these shorts are actually pretty amazing. Good thing they’re so stretchy too, or I probably wouldn’t have fit in them. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, and got an XS because short legs and hips are always super wide on me. Should’ve gotten the Small. The waistband is very tight, causing spillage when I hardly have any meat on my bones anyway, and the skirt panel pulls at the button. BUT, I still think they look incredible -I just won’t wear short tops. The front skirt is so uniquely stylish, flattering, and balances out well with the super short and tight back. I love all the pockets and, overall, the shorts seem very well-made. Also, they’re surprisingly comfortable. This is a great choice of shorts with fashionable flair. They will look fabulous on you. Just, go with your true size.

Cute but they wear terribly I needed a last minute outfit for a themed party and thought these would be perfect. I am “midsize” - size 12-14 and I’m 5’3. I did have to hem them by 2 inches, but otherwise they fit perfectly until I started moving and dancing as the night went on. It started as weird pooching/looseness in the fabric on the tummy and upper thighs, then progressively stretched to the point of having to keep holding my pants up so they wouldn’t slide down while I did anything! There are no belt loops… which is stupid, and there is no elasticity, so the pants will stretch but won’t revert until washing. Not worth the money in my opinion.. I would have returned had I not hemmed them. Perhaps I will add some belt loops with the extra fabric or regift.

I found these and the reviews looked good. Some of them said to order one down so I measured myself and then chose my size and ordered. Well it came 20 minutes ago and here are the pros and cons. Pros: -they feel great -they look great -the back pocket is big and not fake Cons: -there is no front pocket(its not even there and fake) -i didnt read/expect the waist to be elastic but that could be my fault. Because they do stretch a little they tend to fall down when I move so I need to get a belt.(witch is a con in itself for me) More notes: I'm about 5'5" and the bottom doesn't have a lot of fabric on the floor just a little bit about 2 inches.

I’m posing funny in 2 pictures because I wanted to show how much they easily stretch. I got them in four different colors, but I don’t recommend the white pair if you’re a perfectionist. The white pair collects too much fat is and hair to it. It happened to me at work with a scarf. That’s why I gave it 4 stars. If you go by the size chart, then it is true to size and like I said it stretches so it has gift to it. Not trying to be rude but whoever said they run small was in denial about their measurements. They’re as comfortable as my cotton jeggings from Uniqlo. I recommend these jeans especially if you’re curvy like me.

Good quality, but too big and weird fit I almost kept these but the fit is just too weird for me. As you can see in the they are oddly "pouchy" in the front - there's just a lot of fabric in that area that bunches up and creates a weird shape, especially when you sit down. Not for me. That being said I was pleasantly surprised by the quality, and they are quite stretchy and comfy. I'm 5'3", 135 lbs and I got a medium. Definitely bigger than I was expecting so unless you're cool with them being really baggy I'd suggest sizing down.

worth the buy, so cute & comfortable these are so comfortable and slightly stretchy. i love how high waisted they are. i am not one to buy jeans online but these were so worth it. for my tall ladies i am 5,7 and these don’t reach my ankles so keep that in mind before purchasing. :)

Beautiful Gorgeous Stunning This belt is absolutely stunning in person. It almost feels like cheating the system to get it for as inexpensive as it is. The belt is made out of a silver glitter faux leather that is thick and durable and the glitter does not flake off. Each of the rhinestones is riveted into the pleather and is super secure, they aren't going to be falling off. It has heavy duty hardware encrusted with rhinestones, the belt is just heavy weighted and feels super expensive. The belt runs true to size, I have a 38 in waist and ordered the L that fits 39-44in and have plenty of room to adjust if I wear low rise pants or otherwise. I've got the silver belt that has the iridescent rhinestones through the middle, I love the little pop of rainbow that they give. Although the belt says it's western style I actually find that it works well with many styles of clothing and think that this belt is an absolutely beautiful accent to any outfit to make it super fancy.

100% cotton, dry clean recommended The size XL fit my 180 lb, 5'3" body. BUT girls with thick thighs like me won't get that baggy pant look you see on the models. That said the pants are nice. Elastic leg bands fall just above the ankle and the button detail is a nice touch. I didn't realize that detail faces forward, not to the side like I thought, but it's fine. Also from the pictures, I thought the pockets were wider/bigger which isn't a problem but just pointing it out. Fabric has a chemical smell so you need to wash before wearing. I'm holding my breath they don't fall apart in washer since professional dry cleaning is recommended for these 100% cotton pants, but the tag also says wash separately. I have to learn to read descriptions more carefully, but when I saw them I just had to have them, especially for only $19!! Ordered another pair in XXL to see if I get that baggy leg look.

Lightweight & Comfortable 5'2" 165lbs: Ordered Large - My new favorite summer pants. These are the perfect length and the waistband is so comfortable. I like a low waist and these pants stay where you put them. The can definitely be worn higher on your waist. The material is the perfect thickness to provide a little protection from a breeze, but lightweight enough to wear on a hot sunny day. The button detail is a really nice feature and gives these an upgraded comfy clothes look. I also love the loose fit elastic at the bottom of the legs. I wanted a pair of crop jeans for spring and summer. I’m tired of finding crop pants in regular length that I would have to shorten them. These were 6P and the length is perfect, about 4” above my ankles and just below the wide part of my calf. Mid calf is not flattering on my since mine are muscular not slender. The fabric has some stretch as well as the waistband. They are comfortable and flattering. I first got them inn 4P but they were a little snug so I exchanged them for the 6P. My others in 4P were fine size wise. I’m 5’3” and 125 lbs. the smaller size would have stretched out eventually, but I wanted them right away. Note: the fabric has some slight imperfections placed purposefully near top front of thighs for the distressed look.

Order your normal size I live for leopard print! The first time I ordered this jacket Amazon had suggested that the large would fit me best, so I ordered up from my normal size medium. I assumed that maybe this piece ran small. Nope…it was WAY too big for me, and not in a cute, oversized way. So I returned it and ordered a medium. Still oversized, but it actually looked adorable. I think I definitely could’ve sized down and still had plenty of room to move around in. It’s meant to run a little large though, so if you’re looking for a snug fit then order a size (or two) down. Otherwise go with your regular size and you’re golden. One thing I noticed is that the print is clear and defined. Sometimes the images/prints on clothing are a bit blurry, but this one looks pretty pristine. The price point isn’t too hefty…especially for denim, and the material isn’t itchy or incredibly stiff. I wouldn’t suggest wearing it in cooler weather unless you really plan on layering up. I love that it can be styled in so many different ways…with jeans, leggings, skirts, trousers, tees, sweaters, dresses, button-ups, etc. I feel like the possibilities are endless. For reference I’m 5’3”, 152lbs, 36C and ordered a medium. The only reason for the 4 stars rather than 5 is because the first medium I received had a broken button.

Wrong measurements I got these for Christmas from a family member and I swear I thought she had sized them down when ordering. But no it is just lying about the measurements. I actually have both pairs of jeans now and the flower embroidered one is great they are very comfortable, stretchy and I’ve had them for about 5 months and they are still not even worn out a little. The star ones however- in the same size might I add- are terrible. Not stretchy or comfortable, in fact, after spending 5 minutes trying to get them on, I measured them and the waist was the only slightly correct size and I’m really dissatisfied and upset. I got medium and it says the waist is supposed to be about 39 inches but when I measured it it was 32 at most I honestly don’t remember how much the out-seam was but I measured 38. I admit I’m a little curvy at 5’4”, 150lbs but this can’t be right. I’m upset because I can’t even return them since they were a gift and I don’t have the receipt.

These are like leggings not jeans and are stretchy. The dark blue are not blue Updated review.I ordered gray and dark blue. I recieved the dark blue and they are not dark blue at all. They look to me as if these are a purple. A light purple so I contacted amazon bc I really thought that they mistakenly sent the wrong color. So amazon sent me out another pair. I recieved them today and these are exactly the same color as the ones that I still have to return back to them. So just letting people know that they are not dark blue. Would be very cute if they got the dye correct and actually made them dark as shown in the picture. This is not a camera picture color difference. It's a huge difference in color and not even blue a all.. I posted 2 pics of what they say is dark blue. The gray ones are actually gray. Not dark gray but gray. I took 2 stars away bc of this and the annoyance plus having a return them back to UPS for refunds because of their mistake. I like these a lot. They are comfortable and easy to move around in bc they stretch . I've read reviews about broken pockets or rips in them. Mine came in just fine . I have no issues with the ones that I have received. They are not buttlifting at all . I can't remember if they were advertised as being so but they are not. I'm going to be using these as gym pants bc that is what they look and feel like . Oh and all pockets are real usable pockets . I'm 5"5 and these are just at my ankles so not tall girl friendly at all. They are not see thru either. They are a little tight around my stomach right now but that’s because I’m about 10 pounds heavier than I normally am so I think when I am back in shape soon, these will fit perfectly! I would certainly recommend them if you are also into this late 60s era of clothing because the pattern and the colors are spot on. I hope this review is and you have a fantastic day!

Great length for tall(er) ladies This review is for the “ Womens High Waisted Jeans Comforts Straight Leg Loose Stretchy Lightweight Tummy Control Trendy Jeans for Women”. I am tall 5’10” with a long inseam and always have trouble finding pants to fit me. These showed a 42.5” total length and when I saw the photos showing the large bottom seam stitching I got them. They have almost 2.5” extra material in the big bottom seam. I let the seam out and stitched them to the longest possible length. And yay, I got tall jeans! These jeans are high waisted and have wide legs. They are stretchy in the right places and are very comfortable, I washed and dried them and they did very well. I am not a big fan of the “whiskers”, they are more pronounced than in the listing pictures. It does not feel like they any tummy control All in all these are good jeans. The competing market is huge and prices range from $15 to $1500. At $41 they are a decent buy if you want wide leg, high waist and stretch.

Right amount of thickness and stretch Fit/Sizing - I am 5'8" tall, U.S. women's size 6 (usually) or 28 jeans, long torso. Checked size chart (garment measurements), my waist/hip measurement is between the size 6 and 8. Ordered size 6 expecting a little stretch. Fits comfortably snug in waist, hip and upper half of my butt, falls fairly straight down from there. My suggestion- order the size that includes your measurements at, or up to the next size. For example, order size 6 if your waist is from 26.8"-28.3". Order size 8 if your waist is 28.4"-30", order size 10 if your waist is 30.1"-30.7", and so on. These are very high waisted even on my lanky torso; when I sit down the waist hikes up above my belly button. I like that just fine, but something to consider if you are more petite. Legs are shorter than I'd prefer which is expected, because I am too tall for regular sizes and too short for special tall sizes. It's super annoying but I'm sure there would be more complaints if they were longer so can't exactly blame the pants. Materials/Design - Maybe the other colors use different denim, but I wouldn't call this thick, scratchy old-school denim. I'd describe it as midweight, slightly stretchy but holds its shape well all day, and relatively smooth. Not crazy about the whiskers, but not a dealbreaker. Considering how many pairs of jeans I have to try on in a store before finding one I like, was pretty surprised at the quality and fit of these. I take time to write honest reviews that I would find , whether favorable or not.

They are flexible and great for daily wearing. The one thing I am iffy about is the high waist of these is *really* high, like almost to my chest high. I have a short torso so I am sure that is why. Very Comfortable Denim Dress with Pockets! This was a very comfortable denim dress that was not too stiff and not too flimsy. Overall, the quality was better than I expected. The buttons work perfectly, being snug enough to hold the denim together and yet easier enough to button and unbutton. I'm 5'8" and I thought the length was perfect. It wasn't too short or too long. I really liked the deep pockets that could easily hold a phone, keys, or small wallet. The pockets are somewhat hidden on the sides and designed in a manner that does not stand out. A quick look at the dress and you might miss them. The sleeves were long enough to fit my longer arms and the elastic wrists helped prevent them from being too long. Overall, the dress has a baggy feel and is free flowing. In some ways this can be flattering as it hides your stomach. Other times, the extra baggy look can make you look bigger. I'm not sure that I would call it flattering, but it's not bad. Overall, I did like this dress a lot. It strikes a lot of balance and I feel it can be versatile. I do recommend this dress for most anyone looking for a denim dress. I really like the way the collar dresses up the look, too.

Feels unfinished and weird cut to it. I am 5'7" and 160lbs with a 31" waist I normally wear between a size M and L, I got this in a size L and it was slightly oversized.This gave me Free People vibes and looks to be a dupe of one of their other shirts but I really wasn't impressed with how it looked on or the quality. The edges are unfinished and did have a small line of stitching to prevent further unraveling, but the unfinished edges didn't look intentional...they just looked sloppy.The fit to this is weird. The arms were really long and there are no buttons or elastic so you can't roll them up and have them stay up. The material has no give to it, so you can't push them up your arms either. The cut was very oversized but strangely short in length and the slits up the sides were oddly placed. I just thought it was a very unflattering and non-functional piece.The material is a think but strangely stiff denim, a stiffness I might expect out of a thicker denim but not out of a demin this thin. It was uncomfortable to wear and unflattering. I won't be keeping and would struggle to recommend this, especially at this higher price point. This looked and felt cheap to me.

The Vetinee Women's Overalls Denim Loose Fit Straight Leg Bib Overall Jeans are the cutest and most comfy bib jeans I have ever owned and that’s saying a lot. I’ve been a bib jeans wearer for many years. As an artist and creator, bib jeans are the best to wear when working and creating. These bib jeans have some nice stretch going on, so no matter what position you’re in, sitting or standing, they are comfortable. The great thing is, they don’t continue to stretch out throughout the day. They maintain their shape and fit, nicely. According to the manufacturer’s size chart and my body measurements, I should have purchased a XXL but I did not. I went with an XL instead so the fit on me isn’t as baggy as on the model in the listing’s photographs. I wasn’t looking for baggy. I have a few pairs of those already. That said, I’m quite happy with the fit and I do love these bib jeans! For sizing reference: My Bust measures 43", My Waist Measures 38", and my Hips Measure 48". I’m 5’5” tall. I ordered the XL.

I've always wanted a pair of overalls so I was excited to find these and try them. I love them and don't know why I waited so long to get a pair. The fabric on these is really comfortable. Though it has the look of denim, it is a soft denim with a mix of cotton, polyester and spandex. The spandex makes them stretchy and the cotton makes them soft. These fit perfectly. I ordered a medium and usually wear a size 6-8. I'm 5'3" and the length is a bit long but I was planning on rolling them anyway so it's not a big deal. The best part about these is I don't have to worry about bending over and having my whole back side, including my butt crack show while I'm doing yard work. And they look cute for wearing around too without working in them. Overall, I can't find anything I don't like and would recommend them for anyone who is looking for a comfortable pair of overalls.

Super cute I’m 5’5” 147 lbs 38D and got these denim overalls in a small. The fit is spot on (I prefer something a bit more fitted), the length is just right for someone my height. Large side pockets, fits my iPhone 13 Pro Max. Looks great with a t-shirt or cami. I have to take 1 button off in order to take these on/off because my hips are pretty large. The fabric is easy care, I just wash it like my denim jeans. I prefer to air dry but do like to put it in the dryer for a few minutes after it’s fully dried to soften it up. At the current listed price of $40, I think it’s a pretty reasonable price. Good quality.

Aaaaamzing!!!! This is seriously the most amazing pair of jeans! To start, I live in the Northeast where winters are long and cold. i have owned a few different pair of flannel-lined jeans that I paid MUCH more for than the listed price of these jeans. It's early but my initial opinion is that I like these better. When I took them out of the package, I thought they looked small so I tried them on right away. I'm generally a size 12 with most of my weight around the middle. I ordered large and they fit perfectly. Instant love! I wore them all day. There's just enough stretch to them to accommodate the places where it's needed without being baggy in places where you don't. At 5'4", I'm on the shorter side and the length is perfect for me. Note that they may be a little short on a taller person. The lining is soft and warm but not overly heavy so that you can transition from outdoors to indoors without overheating. The zipper and the button are sturdy and well constructed. One of my other favorite qualities of these jeans is the boot cut. It's logical that if you are wearing flannel-lined pants that you would also be wearing a bootie/boot yet other high end brands of flannel lined jeans typically have a straight or tapered leg which prevents that. This is one of the BEST items that I have ordered online!

Perfect jeggings and true to size Oh WOW these are amazing and I’m already scanning for a second pair. I am 5’3” 117 lb with a 25” waist and 35/36 hip. My experience is true to size based on my body type and true to color. I’d say more but I need to go shop for a second pair of these!

Finding the Perfect Fit: A Review of the White 2-Piece Denim Set Let me share my experience with this white 2-piece denim set—it's a tale of size surprises and durable style. As someone who stands at 5'9" but typically orders larger sizes to avoid pants being too short, I opted for a size large. The result? Well, the pant length is spot-on, but the fit elsewhere is, shall we say, roomy. The waist and top are especially loose, thanks to my underestimation of the size and fit. But let's talk about the positives. The material is top-notch—durable, non-transparent, and heavyweight. Plus, the solid stitching and hems throughout ensure longevity and quality. Despite the sizing hiccup, I can't deny the potential of this outfit. It's stylish, well-made, and versatile. My advice? Order the right size for a flawless fit. With the correct sizing, I have no doubt this set would be a wardrobe staple. So, if you're in the market for a chic denim ensemble, don't hesitate to give this one a try—just be sure to get your sizing spot-on for the perfect look and feel.

Tall Girl Friendly I love that these jeans are made for long legs. I have a 32.5" inseam and these jeans reach all the way to my ankles. The jeans are comfy and the material is nice. The con for me is that these jeans aren't truly wide leg. They are like a weird cross between straight and wide leg. When I want a wide leg, I like it to be very noticeable and these just look more like straight leg or bootcut. And another con is even though I have a short torso, I usually find high waist jeans look well on me. These don't. the crotch hangs too low (the rise is too long) and they are baggy between the waist and hip area. They are just not flattering on me without the top of these jeans being covered. I ordered according to the waist size on the size chart but I'm guessing I should have gotten my regular size 0/2. I ordered the size 4 and I am 5'10, 118 lbs, 32-25-34 for reference. If you are looking more for a bootcut jean and you have a really long rise then these jeans may just work out well for you.

The perfect boyfriend cut jean Loose throughout the leg and gets softer and looser as you wear them, exactly as should be in a boyfriend cut jean. It is more like traditional denim, there is not a lot of elastic in the material, but the denim is high quality and softens up A LOT. With the loser leg fit, you don’t need the stretch. These photos show wear after 8 hours and how much they can loosen up! Size down one size. I probably could have gone down 2 sizes except that boyfriend cut jeans are supposed to fit loser in the hips and legs in general so it doesn’t make sense going too tight. I’m usually at 27, at 5’3” and usually 128lbs with curves in hips and thighs, but the 26 fit perfectly. Bonus points: they are very comfy while pregnant! I’m currently 5 months pregnant too and , while my waist size hasn’t changed drastically yet the 26 is still the right size. Since there is a tiny bit of stretch material in them, I probably have easily another inch around the waist I can grow into if pushed.

Tall plus Girl Okay so first off I didn’t pay for expedited and it still came the next day! When it says earliest delivery date, most times Amazon pushes through and it’s COVID-19 so I’m shocked. I am 6 foot I weigh 280 pounds and I’m thick thighs no butt. For the thick thighs women with no butt is best to get your exact size because we want to also flatter our butts just as much as are thighs. If your thighs are bigger than your butt or vice versa to an extreme amount probably get one size bigger but it may be a little loose. I like tighter fit jeans in my thigh area to complement my hips and thighs also because I have a small butt once again lol! And you also know the more you wear jeans they stretch. So I know that my thighs will start to make room for themselves if I need any more LOL.These are perfect. They stretch but you can tell There’s some denim in it because it doesn’t have an extreme stretch. These are not jeggings! They are the regular polyester plus size material for big girls I just have jeans are being right now with a little stretch but I still denim feel! I don’t mind that lip space under the abdomen because I know my thighs are the reason why! Also it goes all the way down ! No high water !!!!

Almost perfect I like how stretchy the material is. Thick enough. I love the flare as well. For your reference: I am 5'2", US Medium. 160 lbs. Somethings can be improved in the design. Adding some side pockets would be really nice because it would really make it look like real pants instead of jeggings with elastic waistband. I kind of like to tuck my shirt in my pockets. The waistband is a bit too tight. These are high-waist pants and it being so tight around the waist is restricting because I have GERD it messes up my stomach because it pushes my stomach too much. For that reason I can't wear it as much as I want to. If the waist band is at least 2 inches loose, these would be comfortable. I like that the hemline is raw so I was able to cut these to match my height and not worry about sewing. Because I am short, the hole is not hitting me at the knees, but it doesn't bother me. The only thing I really would say are not very good at not having pockets and the waistline being super tight. Other than that, I would have give these 5 stars because they are cute! If you are tall and skinny, you would love these!

Sturdy & flattering, run small I'm fond of these jeans. I love a straight leg and I love a patch pocket, so I was already primed to like them. I was a little apprehensive about them being only 85% cotton, but they feel like true denim to me (unlike some more polyester-y feeling jeans.) The "classic blue" color really is a classic. I love that they're a solid wash without any whiskering or fading (which I tend to despise.) I only wish they had a little more slackness in the thighs, because they do cling to the tops of my thighs a little bit, which is typically not something that happens with a straight leg. But it's not too bad; I'm probably my own worst critic where my upper thighs are concerned. They hold my tummy pooch in rather than emphasizing it, which is nice. I am a size 10 with wide hips, and ordered the large. The waistband is snug, so these definitely run on the small side. I'm 5'5" and the hem falls just above my heel in the back. I usually prefer an ankle length, but these jeans are the perfect full length, which is better for wintertime anyway. I think they will be a smidge more comfortable after I lose just a couple pounds, but I am pleased with these jeans and anticipate wearing them a lot in the months to come.

I’m liking these! Fit me well. I like these Dokotoo cargo-style hiking shorts more than I thought I would. They fit me pretty well, and I like that the waistband is tie-style, it’s stretchy and adjustable. The material itself has some stretch and give, it isn’t completely stiff but don’t expect it to stretch a whole size or anything. For reference, I’m about 130lbs at 5’5”, and my measurements are 36”/28”/38”. I usually wear a size 8 in pants, though I recently lost some weight so I might fit a 6. Medium works well on me! The pant legs are a little larger than my thighs, but I’d rather have them be a bit too big over too small. The only things I’m not as thrilled about is the fact the pockets on the back aren’t real (but the front pockets are and nice and roomy!), and that the care instructions seem difficult. The label says it must be hand-washed cold, and can’t be dry cleaned or tumble dried, and to cool iron if needed. I’m not sure who really has time to hand wash their clothes, but it isn’t me! However I did throw them in my washer on a regular cycle with the rest of my laundry (on cool temp water) and they seemed to come out just fine. I hung them up to dry and it did seem like they wrinkled more than I would like, but that’s nothing an iron can’t handle. Overall I’m liking these shorts and will continue to test and wear them over the summer! So far they’re very comfortable and trendy. I recommend them and hope they will last me at least for the next few seasons!

Why is it so hard to make butt pockets. Just with cargo and side pockets you would expect all to be there, but otherwise nice shorts and would recommend.

Definitely wide leg baggy. I ordered these PLNOTME Women’s Boyfriend Wide Leg Jeans for a healthy purpose. I have osteoarthritis in my knees and plan to wear my new knee braces when my boyfriend and I start walking trails regularly during the cool fall weather. I needed jeans that would allow room for my braces. These jeans are definitely 12" wide leg baggy.I ordered the largest size 14 (size chart measurements: waist 36.6", hip 46.5") because my waist is 38" and hips are 42". Lucky for me, the waist and hips fit well. Other reviewers have said they run small. The waist band is also adjustable with additional buttonholes. I stand 5'4" and already knew these would be long on me, as are most jeans I have ordered. Sometimes, I can roll up pantlegs, but these wide legs won't accommodate. The fabric is not overly heavy, and the stitching appears good. Because they are wide-legged, I was able to slide these on even while wearing my sneakers, and no one will know when I wear knee braces underneath. I did notice a bit of a buckling with the zipper though, which was even more apparent after I washed them.I checked to make sure these jeans were colorfast before I washed them in cold water on a delicate cycle. Then I tumble-dried on medium heat with other jeans but removed and hung them 15 minutes before the end of the cycle. I wore my white shirt that's more form-fitting. I didn't notice any shrinkage in the jeans, other than maybe a half inch in the length. However, the zipper’s buckling was definitely more uneven. I figured out it's better to zip up, first, and then button the waist.Overall, this wide leg style doesn't really look attractive on me because of my apple shape and short height, but these PLNOTME Women’s Boyfriend Wide Leg Jeans will serve a purpose for me to start walking again for exercise so I can start losing this excessive weight.

Soft flowy denim through legs, tight waist. I will preface this by saying I am 5’4” & short-waisted, but these are not mid rise, but high waisted. While the leg is full & flowy (for denim), the waist is very fitted. I am between a 10-12 depending on fit, I always size up to be safe when ordering online. These fit but are tight through the waist. People slimmer than me won’t have this issue. If you are in between sizes, definitely size up. The denim is nice, but no stretch. I feel like a little spandex is put in a lot of pants these days, these are listed as 48% Cotton, 36% Rayon, 16% Polyester, so no give. The model has a cuff in the listing, but the material isn’t stiff enough to hold a cuff. If you want a cuff, extra steps will be necessary. As I am short, I was looking to cuff too. They are a smidge long on me, but not as much as I thought they would be, so they may be short in taller women. That might be a cute look, as long as you know what you’re getting. Love the patch pockets, but I do wish they didn’t have the buttons on the waist band, as I would like to wear with a belt sometimes. Overall, a cute look that is something a little different.

Nice pants! Be aware of the characteristics to make sure they're for you Overall, awesome pants! Pros: - Long enough! Probably even too long (I got large tall) but as a tall girl who rarely finds long enough inseams, I have a tendency to overdo it just to relish in the rare experience of too much length. - super comfy. The outside is smooth fabric and the inside is a thin, soft fleece. Warm enough for cool weather but not so insulated that they're a deep-winter-only pair. - pockets are deep enough, ankles are snug enough to look good without being tight Cons: - the sizing chart was a bit wonky, so out of caution I sized up to a large knowing I'd rather be comfy than sausage-cased. There's a lot of extra room, so I really should have stuck to the usual medium. For reference, I'm 5'10", 160lbs, and typically wear size 8 pants. I have a 33" inseam. - the outside is kinda shiny. Coupled with the extra material, they feel a little like 80's windbreaker pants without the awful swishing sounds The photos are done after washing and drying to see if there was any shrinkage (anyone how has felt the disappointment of bringing home pants that were long enough in the store to end up having yet another pair of slightly too short house pants after the first wash knows why I waited to review) and I didn't detect any shrinking, so that's fantastic for buying true to size. These pants have me excited for autumn weather so I can get cozy in these pants that'll actually keep my ankles warm!

Knee bagging and wrinkles I used to have a high opinion of the Gap brand, but my perspective has changed. I’m 5'4" and 150 lbs, and I ordered a medium, which fit me well. I liked it when I first wore it, even though the material felt thin. However, after just 4-5 wears, the pants started developing knee bagging. It’s also constantly wrinkled when I want to wear it, which is frustrating. The back pockets were sewn shut, but I managed to open them myself. I originally bought these for comfortable wear, but with the baggy knees and constant wrinkles, I lose confidence every time I put them on. Don’t buy these, there are better options out there

Do not size up… Size reference: I am 5’6” and 126 lbs. Almost all of my jeans are US size 4 or 27 and the majority of my elastic waist pants are medium (leggings, jeggings, joggers, sweats, etc.). The size chart suggested a small for sizes 4 and 6 but I decided to order my stretchy pant size of medium. Because of the cotton and rayon fabric, I decided to prepare for a little shrinkage. That was a mistake. Material: Thin, buttery soft, with just a hint of stretch. Lovely shade of army green with an elastic waist, functioning front hip pockets, and drawstring. Opinion: Definitely go by the size chart and downsize if you are on the line. These pants are so large on me that I can’t even cinch them with the drawstring to stay on. Way too big in the waist and baggy in the rear and legs. Honestly, the small probably would be extra roomy as well because these are not even close to my other mediums. They seem well made and I love the softness of the material. Definitely not a good choice for snow days but great for warmer weather. If they had fit, I would have used them for business casual attire at the office during the summer.

Go by hip measurement I ordered a Small PETITE in indigo. I am 5 feet tall and wear a size 4. I was hoping they would fit shorter, however they do hit at ankle. Pants fit loosely, I would say closer to a 6 in the waist (chart says 4-6) but because of drawstring in waistline they can be pulled in. My hip measurement from chart is between a size XS and S but because of the cotton/rayon content I was fearful of shrinkage and did not want them to fit tight as these are a more casual look. They did not shrink to a noticeable degree. Material is on the lighter side for pants, I would consider these better for warmer seasons. Very comfortable and soft to wear. I disagree with some reviews that say pants have stretch. There is no elastic in material, and I don't consider cotton/rayon to be stretchy. Front pockets fit my entire hand (or cell phone), however back pocket is shorter.

Nice Quality, but size up I’m actually surprised at the quality of this skirt. It’s a thick denim, not the lightweight stuff – do remember that because this skirt doesn’t flow softly and it might be a little stiff for some. I purchased both the grey and dark blue denim in this style and love them both. The buttons are fixed on well, the seams are good, and they seem like any other pair of well made jeans (other than it's a skirt!). You will most likely want to size up, unless showing you ‘assets’ is something that appeals to you. I’m in my 60’s, and I’m 5’5”, 107 lbs and quite fit. I ordered a size Small. Not Extra Small. I’ve a tiny waist, and this skirt still fits well. But it clearly hugs my butt. Not something I’m keen on in this part of my life. I’m showing it with a cashmere sweater tucked in (it’s rather thin), and also with a sweater worn on the outside. The skirt does look far better when it’s ironed – I washed and ironed both (no, neither skirt shrunk in a warm wash and regular dryer) and they do look far nicer than the photos I’m showing. The bottom of the skirts tend to get stuck in the tops of my taller ankle boots, but not so much in my shorter boots. Again, it’s a stiffer skirt, semi-full, so walking more slowly is required. Well, at least for me! Still, I love my skirts! I think they'll be so nice this winter with a long, warm sweater. I've a pair of fleece lined tights that will look great with either skirt, and keep me warm this winter.

This skirt is medium weight, stretchy denim that hugs your curves. I bought size M, gray color. As you can see, it is a darker gray, and is maxi length on me. I am 5’3” 133#. The medium fits perfectly. Because of the — uh, revealing — fit in the hips, if I wear this to work it will be with a longer top. This will absolutely show off your curvy booty , if you have one. Overall I like the look, but am deducting one star for the cheesy fade marks. The stretch denim makes the fit comfortable and forgiving, and I really appreciate the deep front pockets!

I'm 5'7" and about 125lbs. Pockets lie flat. A bit spendy $$ but a really nice skirt.

Terrible smell! I purchased the 2xl in Blue. I want to love this jacket, since it fits perfectly and is exactly what I am looking for but the smell is so BAD! It arrived with tags on and in a plastic ziplocked bag. Once I took it out it had a horrid odor, like your face being smushed up to a new stack of tires or like it was dipped in chemicals! I washed it with a brand name quality detergent most of us use and lightly dried it with my favorite smelling dryer sheets. Still it smells bad! I then sprayed it with a wrinkle eraser spray that smells like clean cotton and hung it. After letting it hang to air out for two days it STILL SMELLS! For anyone that can overlook the smelliness, the collar is not frayed and the 2xl is the perfect size for anyone that is a size DD, 18 or 20, and 5'10''+. It has a bit of a stretchiness to it, which is nice. However, it is much lighter denim than the ones you get at some other big brand names and stores.

These GENLECK crossover, high waist, cuffed jeans are so on trend right now! I really like the softness of the denim, good pockets and just enough distressing in the wide legs. My husband doesn't care for what he calls "the high water" look, however, when the trend is over you can clip a couple stitches for a regular fringed length. The waist has the regular button closure and a hidden button underneath. Now the sizing... For reference I'm 5'6" 128# ~ waist 30, hips 36. When I ordered I noticed 2 size charts and went with the one recommended by Amazon for a 30" waist, that chart showed sizing measurements from GENLECK ~ so I went with M. However, that chart had different size options according to style, I missed that originally. The waist sizing was smaller on the chart at the top of the page, but with the stretch (and these have good stretch) I figured M should work. When I got them, I noticed the tags had 2 different sizes labeled S and M! I'm not sure what size I received, doing my own measuring they seem to be the S from the top chart. They really are cute, but watch the sizing...

This is soooooooo cute!!!! I am 5'6 and 170 lbs and I got a medium. The length is a little long on me but the waist was a perfect fit.I am not sure if it is very flattering on me as it looks a little long and and wide on me BUT i find it very very cute! So who cares what other people think? lolThe color is really nice and I love the added touches with the "worn" marks on the pants. It is also very very stretchy!! SOOOOOOO FREAKING COMFORTABLE!! Comfortable with a Flattering Cut It’s always hit and miss for me to order clothing online, but this GENLECK wide leg capri jeans are a great find. Material is stretchy in all the right places, and the off centered zipper is a cool design feature. I am 5’10 and 165 pounds and the large fit me well, although I likely could have sized down. The denim is on the heavier side, so it feels more structured and durable but still comfy to wear all day. Length is good, fading and scuffs are trendy, and the pockets are ample. They did arrive with a slight denim smell but it disappeared quickly. I’d advise washing before wearing as the color is rich and saturated and I worry that would bleed onto other fabrics.

SO CUTE!! I literally walked in the room with these and got so many compliments immediately!! People have said stuff about the sizing, but it worked perfectly for me. Im 5’6 and around 145-150 lbs, and i wear mediums for bottoms. These fit perfectly and are so cute! They are flattering, and i get a feel so confident and comfortable in these! I know these dont have the best reviews, but for me these worked perfectly. I have been struggling to find good jeans that fit me well, and these do amazingly!! And for any girls with bigger butts or hips like me, these are amazing for that! Not too tight like others often do. I would say try it, because if you get ones that dont work you can always return it. If it does work, you will have the perfect jeans! Edit: ok so im back a few weeks later, and the only thing i would say, is that for some reason it comes out of the drier smelling burnt?? It did from the first time and i dont know what to do about it. Just be aware and even with this i still am obsessed!

BUY THESE RIGHT NOW I’m 5’2 so these are long, but they won’t make me trip over them for being too long. I’m typically a size 6 in Old Navy jeans & these fit like a glove!!! I’m literally in love, they feel nice & not scratchy! The color & hole are exactly as pictured too.

Warm legs! A little bulky, but manageable. I'm always cold, so I finally started buying more fleece-lined clothes. These keep my legs warm and I am happy that I bought them. I would prefer a longer inseam though! -Sizing- I’m 5’3” and I bought "Elasticwaist Blue88" in medium when I was wearing about an eight in pants, but I couldn't get these on at all! The next winter I was down to a four or six - and these finally fit. Several reviewers said these ran large, but my experience is that the medium runs small. -Washing- These machine wash and dry easily, without any noticeable shrinking. Colorfast on the outside, fluffy warm on the inside. I wash these with my dark clothes. -Wearing- Thick fleece = bulky. The bulk isn’t unreasonable, but it is visible without a jacket. These pants are fairly stretchy, but my issue is the length. Walking is fine, but sitting gets uncomfortable because the pants are really tight on my kneecaps. So I pull these up to get more slack at the knees -which makes driving more comfortable- but then my ankles get cold because they've lost their cover. A longer inseam would be great. Until then I just need to be mindful about socks or boots to compensate for cold ankles at the expense of knee mobility. -Overall- Great buy! I can finally go outdoors in the winter and not freeze immediately. A longer inseam would be nice, but these are still worth buying if you’re always cold like me.

I’m 5’6, ~150lbs and pear shaped. I originally got the medium but returned it for a large because I wanted the legs to be a little longer. When I washed the large pants the legs did shrink a little bit, but so long as I wear boots or high tops they don’t look like high waters. The pants are extremely comfortable. My state is currently in the high 90’s for summer and I haven’t had any problems with sweating, so I’d said the fabric is pretty breathable. I’ve seen pictures of the pants dressed down, but I wear them to work so they’re easy to dress up with a nice shirt or sweater as well. The pockets are deep for women’s pants. You will need to wear seamless underwear or the creases will show through the back, but if you wear seamless they look great from behind. I actually liked them so much I got a second pair in brown 👍🏻

WOW, these are pretty good!! I am really surprised at how good these are! They fit me (almost) perfectly and they look super cute! I have them on straight out of the package in the pics so they are holding some wrinkles still, but overall, excellent pants and I will be ordering in more colors! Some thoughts below. - Fit is true to size with some flexibility. I am 5'7" and 145lbs and holding on to some extra holiday weight! I ordered my true size small and they are slightly tight but its only noticeable at the pleats since they are not laying flat!! Otherwise, these fit perfectly! The back of the waist has elastic which makes these flexible in terms of sizing. I would say order your true size. - The material is not the softest but its not uncomfortable either. I am in Michigan and they don't have a ton of warmth so not sure these will see much wear until it gets back into the 40's or 50s around here. - I LOVE the jogger look for a business outfit. It adds some fun to the outfit and they are flattering! They could easily be worn with sneakers for a more casual but chic look too! - Love that they have pockets and belt loops! Overall, these are shockingly so good. I will be buying in more colors. True to size.

Love The color and design of these pants are great. They are made well. The material is good not thin and has a softer texture to them. They fit true to size. I like that I can wear these with heels to dress up or tennis shoes to wear casually. I picked these up for our Europe trip. They do have some stretch in the waste for a little flex and just a slight bagginess for comfort. I got the large I'm 170 at 5'7"

Love these! Super comfy pants that are extremely versatile. They are loose/more relaxed fit, but aren't baggy looking on me which is exactly what I was looking for. For context, I bought the size small (I'm 5'7" and about 125lbs). I think these would probably be more flattering and comfortable on people who have a waist smaller than their hips. These pants could totally be worn with sneakers or dressy shoes...I wore them to work today with sneakers and a blazer and I felt super comfortable and put together. I would have given these five stars except that the type of material on the outside seems to be picking up every fluffy thing they touch...maybe a lighter colour would be better for that but on the black it shows, so if you buy the black, have your lint roller handy! Overall, I just love these pants and am seriously considering purchasing a second pair in a different colour!

The length is ok on me, in fact if I uncuff them I could even wear them as a full length pant if they were not so tight! For size reference I am 5'4" 150lbs, pear shaped and typically wear a size 8 or 10 in pants. In the picture I am squeezed into the size 8 in black. I don’t even think the size 10 would work. Feels like lounge but looks like work. Size down! Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and can wear either small or medium in stretchy pants. Based off prior experience, I ordered small from Amazon Essentials because they tend run big. The fit of the small is still a little big, similar to what a medium in brick and mortar store brands would run. The smooth elastic waist has a little gap when I’m standing. I have shorter legs and the leg openings touch the top of my feet. I actually like the looser fit because if they were skin tight I think they would look more like sweats than slacks. Description: I really like how these feel. The stretch of yoga pants but made with better material so I can pass these as business casual with the right top. The tag will have to be cut out because the corner does scratch my lower back. Also, and this is an issue I have had from AMZ brands before, the black looks slightly faded compared to other dress pants. It makes it hard to pair with other black pieces from my closet because the difference is quite noticeable. Other than that, I like these comfy work pants. Deducted a star for color and scratchy tag. (Didn’t deduct a star for fit because the size chart does show small to be about 2 inches larger in the waist than standard small).

These sweatpants are huge! I ordered a women's medium. I'm only 5 ft and weigh 130 lbs. I know I'm short, but I wear a medium, sometimes a large depending on the clothing. These pants are gigantic. They come all the way up to my chest and are super super baggie on me. I gave them to my 6 ft tall husband and he can actually wear them around the house because he's pretty skinny. Great value and nice enough to wear in public I liked these palazzo pants so much I ordered a second pair in black and white polka dot. They are soft and drape nicely from the hip. They are a true palazzo pant, which I like, but buyers should be aware that they are "flowy." I am 5'6" and weigh 155 lbs. I normally wear an XL but got the XXL as prompted by Amazon. They are very long and almost touch the ground. They fit well but I think the XL would have been fine as they are stretchy. They don't look like a pajama bottom and you could easily wear them to run errands, grab lunch etc. Highly recommend!

Best Pants for C Section Surgery and just all around comfortable lounge pants! I love these lounge pants! I'm currently 36 weeks pregnant, and with just 2 weeks to go until my C section, I've been living in these. I originally ordered 2 pairs, but I just ordered a 3rd because after my surgery I know I'll need loose pants that don't constrict my incision, and I have a feeling I'll only be wearing these at home for the first few weeks of recovery. I wear a size medium before and during pregnancy. I'm only 5' 3", so these do run a bit long on me. But I only wear them around the house with slippers so I don't mind one bit. With how loose fitting these are, I have no doubt I could wear a size small but I'm fine with them. They're loose enough that they don't constrict my waist, but I can also pull them about halfway up my preggo belly and they'll fit there comfortably, which I really need for after my C section. I wish I'd bought these for my first baby during my surgery recovery, and i highly recommend them before, during, and after having a baby even if you don't plan to have a C section. The weight I've gained during this pregnancy seems to be all baby, so I know I'll still fit comfortably in these after the baby comes. I may have to roll up the waist though. Highly recommend!

Size up I'm 6ft tall 215lbs I have a nice foundation type body... my legs are long AF I GOT THE XL my first ones and they were to short... my second ones I got 2xl and they still ran a little flood but the 3xl are perfect in length... I'm a size 12 in jeans and these are soft and flowy and I freaking love them in the 3xl.... the xl fit me perfectly in size except length so I sized up and they're perfect for lounging and looking cute

Different patterns= Very different types of fabric First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

My normal size M/L at 8 months pregnant I got the 3XL There are a lot of these same looking pants sold all over Amazon. I ordered some by the brand LUBERLIN and I definitely think the fit of these AMiERY ones are better. Be aware: The sizing on these pants is not traditional US sizing!! The 3XL runs waaaay small but not *AS* small as the LUBERLIN ones. For reference, I am 5'8 and typically wear a size 8/10 or M/L. Currently, at 8 months pregnant I wear size M in Motherhood Maternity jeans and in lounge wear I size up to a L. I decided to order the 3XL of these pants and they fit me comfortably over or under my belly. But they will definitely be way too big postpartum. I plan to order the 2XL for postpartum. If you are a true 3XL you will be disappointed by the fit of these. The material of the pants is very thin & light. The color Black #2 has a bit of "sheen" to it almost looking like a sateen, but it doesn't feel like sateen-it feels like jersey knit.

Great pants in all sizes! Not see through! I love these pants! I bought them for my sisters and mother, a variety of sizes and body types. They fit all of us and I just bought the sizes I would regularly assume we wear. In the photo the sizes I bought and our measurements are listed from left to right; size small (she is 5’2 and 120 lbs), size xxl (5’8 and 260lbs), size medium (5’5 and 160), and me wearing a medium (5’4 and 145). One of us had a full pair of black pants underneath in this photo and at least one other was wearing black underwear and you can’t tell, which is awesome! They are long on all of us, and there is a large waist band, the second photo shows me with the waist pulled up all the way covering my belly button and meeting my bottom rib, I probably could have gone with a smaller size as they are quite loose and really stretchy but I’m happy all the same :) the fabric is cool, I can cuddle my dog and roll around with him and they don’t get hairy 🙏🏻.

Comfy and VERY long These pants are about as expected, they're soft and comfy, very baggy and I like the high rise that is not elastic. The print is not great, it's obviously a printed image that looks kind of cheap(and it is cheap), but they're just lounge pants so it doesn't bother me much. The print is still pretty. I am very short, 5'1", and I'm used to pants being too long, but these were WAY too long. I cut 4 inches of fabric off the bottom, hemmed about 1/2 inch, and they still pool on the floor. One picture is with 1 leg hemmed and 1 leg with the original length. The other 2 more full size photos are after the pants were hemmed. I'm about 110lb and I got a small.

Comfy, high-waisted, thin material - perfect house pants! I really love these pants. The material is thin and somewhat stretchy, and they are very comfortable! I'd be happy wearing these around the house any time of year (in California). I was looking for some comfortable lounge pants to wear at home and was delighted to find such cute ones! The high waist is fine, not my favorite, but I love the colors and the length is great on me. I'm 5'8" and about 145 lbs and I got a large, and they are a perfect fit. Now I can be lazy in style with these cute, comfy pants! I might just need some more pairs...Edit: Five years later I still wear and love these pants! I don't wear them every day but I wear them plenty and they have held up nicely. I have since had a baby and put on some weight and now weigh about 170 lbs, but I bought another pair in large last week and it's as comfy as ever! I love these pants so much!

Nice quality, but fit long as expected Purchased for lounging around the house or as pajama pants. They're very comfortable! The material feels soft, and feel thick enough that patterned or bright colored underwear might not be too noticeable through the white stripes. I bought a size small, I'm 5'4" w/ an average build and they are long for me as I expected. Although the waist is adjustable, the fit in the waist is fine, but I will have to fold the waist band over for a better fit. Even folded these are a couple inches too long for me. But I am pleased with the quality! I do wish an x-small size was available!! Purchased a second pair with a different design, just as happy!

Waist is good, long in length I'm a size 12/14 or XL usually in regular clothes. I ordered a size XXXL based on their chart and reviews. I like them and wear them plenty. I could have gotten away with a XXL, but I'm perfectly fine in a 3XL as well; they're not falling down or anything if I tie the string. I'm 5'7" and these are looooong. I can wear them with flats, but they have to be pulled up a bit high on my waist (they do have a decent length crotch too though so you don't get a camel toe when you do that lol) You can dress these up or down. I wear them just about everywhere. Pair with a sheer blouse for work or with a tank top for the store. Very silky and light enough for summer in the mid-south. I got 2 colors and will be buying more.

Comfortable and beautiful! I have been seeing pants like these for several months on Pinterest and they just seemed too good to be true. Cute, affordable, and comfy- could I really have stumbled upon the trifecta of pants? I did! I am 5’7” and as I’m on the curvier side and have long legs, I ordered the XL. They fit perfectly and the pattern looks just like the pictures. I know they’re intended to be more of a lounge pant, but the print is so beautiful I think I want to dress them up. With a fitted top and nice sandal these might be cute dresses up. They’re so light they’ll do well in warm or cool spring or summer weather. I hope they hold up. I’ll likely wash them in cold on the delicate cycle and line dry. If they survive their first few washes, I’ll be buying these for every woman I know.

Nice pants They’re definitely a silky material, which is what I was expecting from the reviews I had read before I bought them. I don’t know if I’d buy again, but I’m glad I got these ones. I’m 5’7” and weigh about 140lbs and I wanted them to be long, which they are. The string is super long. The small is a fine length for my height, though I wouldn't want them to be any shorter. Overall, great PJs, just go down a size!

Best pants I’ve ever gotten from Amazon These pants are fantastic. They fit me (short legged 5’4” and apple shaped) perfectly without the legs dragging on the ground at all. Size DOWN at least 1 size! 5'2" - 165-170 lbs - 41"-36"-41" - White is lined! Hot weather approved. TLDR — Size down at least 1 size, if not 2. Length is good for some petites (5'1" to 5'3") and regular height (up to 5'7"). I'm 5'2" and weigh between 165-170 lbs. depending on the time of the month. My measurements are 41" Bust - 36" Waist - 41" Hips. I generally wear size 10-12 or L-XL depending on the brand, fabric, type of clothing, etc. I have a small FUPA but no butt, so occasionally I can size down to a size 6-8 or M (Medium) depending on the stretch of the fabric. I'm short-waisted and usually wear mid- or high-rise with a short or regular inseam depending on the pants and type of shoes I'm wearing. I ordered size L (Large) as suggested by Amazon in the color white, and it's too loose and baggy overall. The smocked waistband is comfortable and super stretchy. The white linen pants have lining inside all the way down except 6" from the bottom so it's not see through! You can't see the deep pockets... maybe very faintly if you're looking really hard in the picture. (I wish other white pants are lined like this, because there is nothing more annoying than having white pants that are practically transparent where you can see the pockets or even your nude underwear.) Because the pants are lined, it does add some noticeable weight to the overall feel of the pants, and along with the super stretchy smocked waistband, it causes them to slowly slide down. The pockets are deep and a great addition, and you could fit a phone in there, but because of the overall "heaviness" of the fabric and lining, the phone would definitely pull down the pants because the waistband won't support it. For this reason, I would size down to a size Medium for a relaxed fit or even a Small for a tighter fit. I'm only 5'2" and have the smocked waistband pulled above my belly button. The length of these pants are 41.5" with an inseam of 27.5". I'm wearing 2" platform sandals where the pants are almost grazing the floor. If you're shorter than 5'2" and don't want to wear 3" heels/ wedges/ platform sandals, these may be too long for you. Check the size chart for the overall length of the pants. According to the tag, this is made in China and it's only 30% linen and 70% rayon. It says, "Dry clean only. Do not bleach. Steam iron." I used a garment steamer to get the wrinkles out. The fabric isn't scratchy like typical linen since this isn't 100% linen. It felt light and breathable, even in hot, humid 92°F weather. Sitting on them obviously causes them to wrinkle in the back but the front remains mostly wrinkle free. Based on the size chart, I think going by the hip measurement would be best. Size medium for me would provide a much better fit where the waistband is hopefully a little tighter but the pants are still relaxed enough, while size small would provide an overall tighter fit. I'm debating on washing these to shrink them down a little as others have mentioned. Aside from the dry cleaning care instructions, who has time for that?! I do like these pants a lot and will order another pair or two in different colors and smaller sizes. I'll update my review later if I remember.

Perfect travel, dress-up/dress down pant. Better than shorts for a classier look. Only thing that helped me survive 95 degree temps in Europe this summer. So loose and breathable, I bought 9 colors! I was so comfortable and it kept the hot sun off my legs. I'm 5'1", 116 lbs. I hemmed them a bit and after washing them in cold and line drying them, they still want to shrink. Stretching them back into shape while wet seems to do the trick. In fact the only reason for four stars instead of five is that the care instructions are not clear that it should be dry clean only. So my recommendation is as if you need to hem them, wash them first and then see how much you need to take off after. If you're tall and they fit you perfectly out of the package then I would definitely just dry clean them.

SUPER CUTE SWEATS! I ordered these ANRABESS Women Wide Leg Baggy Cargo Sweatpants in a size Small for my 10 year old daughter and she loves the way they fit! For reference, she's 5' tall and 105lbs-ish with a 26" waist. Though these pants are a little long on her, she insists that is fashion... and I'm here for it! Here's what we like about these sweatpants: •QUALITY GARMENT: These sweats are well made. The stitching is straight and secure. They feel more like a higher end pair of sweatpants than fast fashion. •SOFT: These sweatpants are made of very soft, cozy material. While they are warm, they aren't extremely thick or heavily lined with fleece. •FASHION FORWARD: The actual cut of these pants is really nice! They are very baggy in the legs, specifically at the knees. But they taper up at the waist, giving them a really good shape. My daughter loves the cargo pockets on the sides. •WASH & WEAR! These pants can be washed, dried, and worn dozens of times without compromising the quality. Mom win! Overall, my daughter is just obsessed with these! She likes to wear them with tank tops and a bunch of accessories (not shown in the picture, but she was just lounging). She also wears them with graphic tees and athletic shoes. I just like that they are affordable, comfortable ad made well. We highly recommend them!

They have side and cargo pockets and draw string for tightening them up a bit. If you are like me and wash your favorites sweats once and a great while these WILL create that " I'm wearing a diaper" look because they will sag off you. Good news is you can wash and dry them without shrinking or fading. They are long so they collect dirt AND they get wet when it rains outside.

nice and comfortable, super flowy! I ordered a large based on the size chart, and found the pants to fit well. These were spot on and while medium would've been fine, the large gave me the nice "paper bag" pant look with the rouching at the top.
