The fabric is medium weight, and is actually a bit ribbed with a pronounced seem in the front (not the back). They did come a bit wrinkled, so I'm not sure how they'll hold up when traveling, although that is what I got them for. They're so comfy though so I'll give it a try on my next flight and see what happens! They feel like my pajama pants, so will be ideal for overnight flights while looking chic. I got the dark khaki and they are actually a bit darker brown, and even a bit greenish than the photo shows. Overall I'm super happy, particularly for the price!

Pretty Style, Soft, and Easy to Wear Anyproud Wide Leg Pants are quite nice, as long as you like this style trousers. The material is soft and it's a good weight (not thin nor overly thick). The pants are not see through. The high waist design is attractive and the elastic waistband provides a good hold without being too restrictive or tight. The tie is honestly too long. I'll probably be cutting both sides to give it a reasonable length. The fabric is free flowing - the pants are in no way form-fitting or styled to accent your figure. I would say these are perfect for casual Sunday afternoons. They look just a tad too much like pajamas (in my opinion) to wear out to run errands or meet friends for coffee. That said, others may be quite comfortable in public wearing this pair. The fit is comfortable and aligns with their chart. At 5'7", the 'Small' hangs down to the top of my feet. I love the color, I love the flowing look, I love the feel of the fabric. This is a nice, good quality, casual pant that is well crafted and well designed.

Super Comfy! I am absolutely loving these sweater pants. They are wide legged, high waisted, stretchy, and comfortable as can be. They have an elastic waist with a drawstring and I would say they are true to size. I weigh 150 pounds at 5’7 and I ordered a medium. I do have longer legs, and these fall at the perfect length. There is a cable knot design that goes down the outside seam on both legs. The material feels like a sweater and is incredibly soft. I’ve worn these pants twice and have not washed them yet, but should they not wash well I will come back and update my review. What I like about them is they can be dressed up or down. They’re perfect For a party but also perfect for lounging. I like the diversity of occasions that I can wear these pants too. They look and feel like a quality pant, even after wearing them. Some pants stretch out weirdly, but these pants are not one of them. Very happy!

👠 Elongated Crotch - Runs True To Size For Reference I am ( 36C 162 lbs 5’4” size med / large ) Love the color on these khaki hue sweater pants, but what is most impressive is the wide range of sizes and lengths that is available. The size inclusivity for individuals who require a shorter inseam is Fantastic ! The only negative is that it is limited to only 2 colors ( black & brown ) I would love to see a wider range of color options. The sizing chart states the petite inseam as 26.8”. I measured mine to be a 27” inseam. The fabric is comprised of 50% Viscose, 28% Polyester and 22% Nylon. The material is soft and has a opaque appearance which falls right in between a knit and a sweater. It is not itchy but does offer a good amount of stretch and sight warmth. The elasticized waistband is 2” thick and offers a working drawstring for an adjustable fit. I did not find these to be a high rise as they sit right on the waist on me. What I did find is that the crotch area is elongated, which looks unflattering, but can be slightly corrected by rolling up the waistband as an option, which then will effect the length of the pants. The description refers to these as straight leg design, however the pants I received are straight just up to below the knee, it then flares out to a wide leg hem. What gives these pants some interest is the vertical outseam which runs the length of the pants from the waistband to the bottom hem. The seam in my opinion elevates the appearance of these pants so they can be utilized not only for casual or lounge purposes but with a pair of heels can be taken into the work place. Machine washable according to the care instruction label - cold delicate cycle but I found that hand washing has helped prevent shrinking due to the fabric makeup

Silky soft and warm knit pants with a bit of style. These straight leg, business / casual pants are quite comfortable. They are made of a super soft knit material that is a little fuzzy too. I got the X-Large Petite size and they fit well around my waist, but were a little shorter than I prefer on leg length. I am 5' 4", and often pants are too long. Since they had the petite sizes I went with that, but I guess I am borderline between sizes for the sizing of these pants. According to the chart and X-Large in regular would have 42.1" legs and I know those would be too long. Sort of a pickle on that one, lol. They are well made with solid stitching. The pleat down the front gives them a more stylish look for wearing away from home as well as lounging at home. They have a drawstring for waist adjustment, if needed. There are no pockets, though. With the thickness of the material, I thought they would be very warm, but the knit material must breath well as I wore them for 15 minutes or more while doing a couple things in the house and they were just as warm as the thin pants I had on before trying these on. They arrived in a zip bag and folded with them was a piece of tissue paper. Not sure why, but the tissue paper left tiny (pin-head sized) pieces of white here and there on the pants. The paper looked fine with no tears, but somehow it shed onto the pants, lol. Just mentioning as they were very visible on the black pair of pants I got. Over all, I'm recommending these knit pants. They are of a nice quality knit material and well made. Only caution I will say is study the size chart and hopefully the sizes will work better for the next person!

Very Comfortable Jeans! These are a really comfortable pair of jeans. They are in the more current style, being on the shorter side and flared. I have to say I really like the fit. They have a good bit of stretch to them, so the size 18 fit me fine, and that's my usual size for most jeans. I like the color, which is a bit lighter, but it is still what I would consider a standard jean color. They are relatively high-waisted, but due to all the stretch, they are not going to "hold" you in the way jeans with stiff material will. I like the length on me, I'm 5'6 for reference, and they hit just at my ankle. My only pet peeve is that, basically, the entire pair of jeans is standard with no distressing, except for a random tiny spot on one of the back pockets. I think because this is the only spot for any distressing, it looks more like an actual tear or rip than intentional distressing. It's not a big deal, but that's the only thing I don't like about these jeans.

Don't even bother rolling these bad boys down either because you will have created one huge spare tire around your hips. Big enough to keep people out of your personal bubble though, so maybe that's a good thing. The material is very thick and warm which I absolutely love because I am always cold. Its soft on the inside. The pockets are nice as usual. After seeing how big these pants were when I opened the package I though maybe I could use them for period week pants, but because the waistband is so tight, I don't honestly know if that would give me the relief I'd be looking for. These feel pretty sturdy though and as long as I don't gain any inches right below my ribs I should be able to wear them for a long time.

High-waisted at natural waist, baggy sweatpants, loose ankle cuffs, cute with crop top These are nice quality, thick material, well-stitched, heather grey sweatpants. This High-Waisted style fits right at my natural waist. The elastic waist is slightly snug on me, plus there is a drawstring cord. The rest of the pants are pretty baggy all over, including the butt, front/crotch, hips, legs, and loose ankle cuffs. There are 2 good pockets, one on each side, plenty roomy for hands or phone or keys. I usually wear size Small in sweatpants, size 4 in jeans. I have gained a few pounds, so I feel thicker around the middle than usual. But for reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#, and ordered size Small. These sweatpants look better on the online model than me, and look cute with a tight crop top since they are high-waisted and baggy. But I think they are generally comfy, loose sweats. I think my teenage daughter will probably steal them from me! Thank you!

Odd fit These pants have a very strange fit. For context, I'm 5 feet tall and weigh 122 pounds, and I ordered a small. First, the pants have an extremely high waist; I have to pull them up to my chest for the crotch to fit properly. Additionally, the pants are really long, so they might work better for those with longer legs. The most unusual aspect of these pants is that because the material is thin, they are lined with a pair of shorts. These inner shorts are bulky, making it look like you're wearing a diaper or something underneath. It's a very odd look. However, I do like that the pants are soft. Be aware that they shrink.

PERFECT stretchy pants! LOVE these pants! These are EXACTLY what I was looking for. They’re stretchy, flattering and have REAL pockets, ladies. true to size, just ok I am a bit bummed about these pants. Lots of other reviews said they sized up and they fit a BIT large it wasn't a big deal because they had a drawstring. Well....I sized up and they are HUGE and the drawstring does NOT help. For reference, I am 5'7" and 140/145lbs, I usually wear a small or medium in bottoms and sized up to a medium. They are quite large on me. I plan to wash them to see if they will shrink down a bit, so stay tuned there. The drawstring is fully functional. The pockets are large and easily fit my iPhone 12. The material is a bit scratchy and doesn't have a lot of movement, I am hoping the washing will help with that as well. The material is a bit see through in the white. I had on darker panties when trying them on and they are very visible. However, because they are a looser fit, its nothing indecent. You can see the pockets through the fabric. Overall, I would go with your true size as they are meant to be oversized. The pants are just ok overall but hopefully will improve with a wash.

Mixed Feelings These pants are some how too big yet a bit snug at the waist. I appreciate that the expansion is on the side instead of the large band at the front. But that doesn't give much room or comfort at the front because the elastic is at the sides. Pre-pregnancy, I wore a US 6 to 8 in pants so I ordered the medium. They are quite big around the hip and leg area to the point that it looks like I'm wearing the wrong sized pants. The length also is a problem. I'm 5'4" and while I understand they're supposed to be a cropped fit, they just look like high water pants on me. They aren't flattering on me at all. They are, however, pretty well made pants. The denim is thick and sturdy. There are no front pockets because the elastic is where the pockets would be. They do have back pockets so that's good.

My new favorite pants! I'm so pleasantly surprised at how fabulous these pants are. I had an urge for sailor pants and was happy to find these. The denim is high quality, not thin at all and the fit is great. Sailor pants are always flattering. I was worried they would be too big since many of the reviews said they needed to size down, but the Small fit perfectly and the length is great. I'm 5'4" and 115 lbs. I'm turning 70, and wearing them to a party with my high school friends, which was the last time I wore sailor pants which was early 1970's!

Please make an XS! Loved these jeans. Couldn’t believe they weren’t too long for my 4’11” height (with wedges). I’m 112-115 and these jeans were just too big overall. Very stretchy! I was able to pull these on like sweatpants and while I absolutely love the style, length and color, they were just too big. If they had a XS, I’d buy them in every color! They went back.

So flattering!!! These jeans are gorgeous! And they have enough cotton in them to feel super soft, and enough stretch to feel comfortable sitting and walking in them. They are high waisted and have a nice flare that hits the shoe, just like I wanted it to (I’m 5’6”). They wash fine on medium but I let air dry because I don’t want to risk shrinkage in the dryer. I went with my “regular” size, but could have probably fit in the one below, but less comfortably. So I would suggest go with your regular size unless you are hesitating between two, and in that case go with the lower one. Also: the color is gorgeous!!!

Knee length shorts - for real! Finally, some knee length shorts! That is what I really like about them. So much so that I also got them in black and navy. These shorts remind me of the ones we wore back in the day, and I was happy to have found them. There are a lot of different shorts on this listing, so it can be confusing. This review is for the white shorts with the buffalo check print that you can fold up. From my experience, this style is the longest they carry. I found them to run a little loose, and there is elastic in the back. They sent a little belt with it, but I haven’t even taken it out if its package yet. I recommend following their size chart. These white shorts are not see through with nude underwear! I am including a photo of me wearing them today and I tucked the back of my HOT PINK shirt into them so you can see for yourselves. The one thing I personally dislike about these shorts is the brown buffalo check material that is sewn on. Thankfully I can wear it unfolded and not have that show. Unfortunately, it kinda does show through just a tiny bit, although my husband said he didn’t notice. Also it buttons where you fold it up, and the buttonhole is not done that well. I am letting these things slide since I am happy to have a decent pair of white shorts that are long enough. For reference, I am 5’9”, 134 lbs, and I bought size small.

Nice alternative to a skirt or shorts These capris are less high waisted than they are wide legged. I like the more modern, casual fit; comfortable without looking too frumpy. They are 95% cotton, which is rare these days. They did not shrink after washing in cold and hanging to dry. Although I am purposely choosing to roll with the slightly wrinkled look for a more causal vibe. This look is ideal for warm weather when a skirt or shorts could be warn without the concern of showing your legs. Yes please. I ordered a small, although I usually wear a medium or 6 in pants. There really isn't a need for a belt, but it does jazz up the overall look and was a thoughtful accompaniment. The belt is brown 'pleather' and looks surprisingly decent. But with the elastic waist band in the back, a belt really isn't necessary. There are belt loops that run around the entire waistband. I like the simple styling of the two front pockets. but feel the cut in the back makes my rear look a little pancake-like and baggy. I have short legs and am 5'7", 138. The size small is quite ample. Anyone smaller would for sure want an XS, which I do not believe is available. These capris will be a welcome addition to my spring/summer wardrobe. I love a good neutral color and appreciate the simple style, comfort and the modern, wide leg-look.

I would say these are a must purchase for any party!!

Love These! These pants are SO nice. I am really surprised. They have a nice weight. They are not paper thin but also not super thick. Great for hot summer. They are buttery soft. Love these pants!! I love these pants and receive so many compliments while wearing them. They are comfortable and not too long for a short person (I’m 5’3”). They washed well and didn’t shrink. If you aren’t sure, go for it!!

Fun & Frilly Pants! The - Blooming Jelly Women Linen Pants Summer Vacation Outfits Wide Leg High Waisted Pants Palazzo Flowy Pants Trouser 2024 – are fun and frilly pants that make me happy when wearing them. I love the way the fabric feels on my skin and moves when I walk. They’re not the most flattering style of pants for my fuller figure but I don’t care - I like them. The only somewhat negative comment I have is the sizing chart lists the measurements of the pants themselves and not the body. And they don’t seem to be that accurate in my opinion. I ordered the size XL which according to the chart the hip measurement is 42”. When I measured the pants I received while flat, I measured 52 inches at the hip. The X-Large does fit me but if I would like the pants to look on me as they do on the model, more roomy and gathered like a skirt under the elastic yoke area, then I should have ordered a larger size or two, if it were available. Regardless of all that, these pants are lovely and I’m very glad I chose to purchase them. For sizing reference, my Bust Measures 44”, my waist measures 37”, and my hips measure 47”. I’m 5’4” tall. I purchased a size XL.

These pants are just as cute as depicted in the description photos. The material is a thin but opaque, lightweight fabric with a little texture that drapes beautifully and doesn't wrinkle easily. They are NOT linen, as some of the lines in the item description suggest. The bow in front is sewn to either side, and is not a drawstring, while the rest of the waist is smocked and very stretchy. As for sizing, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 170 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in the "large" size, which I think was the right fit for me (see my pics). While I don't think these are the most flattering pants for my apple shaped flabby stomach, they are super comfortable to wear with no pinching at the waist, and I can see myself wearing them often as soon as the weather warms up. Overall, these are cute, comfortable summer pants, and I'm happy with the quality. I would order from this brand again.

Silky trousers I'm a size 6 and accidentally got a large, they fit slightly loose with some slack on the elastic in the back. I think size LARGE would fit sizes 8-10 like the photo but not as brown. Definitely runs a bit small. Front rise 12" Back rise 15" Inseam 28.5" Waist 15" unstretched Looks just like all the photo. Really cute I had doubts when I read other reviewers posts but I was really pleasantly surprised. I disagree that these are not breathable—I am one of those people who tends to run hot always and these pants are flexible, easy to run around and work with and don’t crease easily or get too hot! I think they’re very flattering and as a matter of fact want all the colors! The pleats and belt loops with scrunch back waistband are flattering. For reference: I’m 5’0”, curvy, and typically wear size 10-12. Ordered the large and fits well

Cute and comfy! I love these pants!!! I ordered them for a trip to Amsterdam next month and I wanted pants other than jeans. I figured I'd give these a try because they seemed to get really great reviews and I found a pattern I love so why not give them a shot. When opened the package, the leg part looked a bit big and I thought they would look like Aladdin pants (LOL) but they don't seem so big when they're actually on. They're roomy but not too big and the material is light but comfy and soft-ish.I love how these come in a variety of prints/colors and are super easy to coordinate an outfit! I plan to wear these with a black tank or black tee and sandals (like I'm wearing in the photo) but I could totally see myself wearing a pair of flats or even heels to dress them up if I wanted to. I also think these would be comfy enough to wear all day while I'm sight-seeing, visiting museums, strolling around town, or going to a flea market.I typically dress in darker clothes but wanted something different, something funky for my trip to Amsterdam. I love bohemian, though I never thought I'd actually like these so much! It might be hard to tell in my photo but they are a perfect dark blue and black floral/mandala type pattern (the color is actually called "Mandala Blue"). The pattern looks exactly how I thought it would be (or is even better!) in person!For reference, I am 5'9" and about 180 pounds. The length of the pants is just right, a little above my ankles. Not too long and not too short even for a taller person. My only beef with these is that I think they look better high waisted however doing so gives me a little bit of camel toe which isn't ideal so I have to pull them down a bit more to my hips for them to be more comfortable (or maybe with a different tee or tank they could work better for me high waisted). I wish the waist part was a bit stretchier or longer, which is the only reason why I gave them 4 stars, but this is definitely not a deal breaker. I was more concerned about these being long enough for my liking and roomy in the leg/thigh area which they very much are.Aside from the high waisted fit thing (which is probably more on my body than the pants themselves), I am absolutely obsessed with these! I didn't expect to love them so much but I really do. They are effortless, super cute and versatile! I can't wait to wear these! I'm so glad I took a chance and tried something I might not have if it weren't for my trip. I will definitely be wearing these into the summer well after my trip to Europe! Thanks so much for a great item!Update: washed these once and I am noticing a few lines on these where the print has faded or the black bled through the fabric? Not sure whether I'm explaining right but they are definitely not wet spots from water as they're a straight line. I did not dry these in the dryer, I let them hang up to dry so I'm not sure why there is a blemish in the fabric. My OCD is killing me knowing these spots exist, but I don't think they would be noticeable to anyone else and I still love these pants.

These are my new favorite pants and as soon as I am done writing this review I am promptly ordering more in different colors. Easy to dress up or lounge around in. Fabric is perfect thickness for summer but it does wrinkle easy; nothing a trip through the dryer won’t fix. Says handwash (who has time for that?)but I threw it in a cold wash with the rest of my clothes and they fared well. I am 5’5” and 190lbs (and losing) and the large (10-16) fit perfect and I think they will still fit well as I lose more weight.

These are the best!! Very light weight and comfy. They looked a bit small out of the bag but they have a lot of stretch. I'm 5'3 and 170lbs, got the 10 to 16 size. I love how they cover my fluffy spots. The best part is they have POCKETS!! And not crappy little ones, I can fit my phone in them. As soon as I put them on I went back and ordered 3 more. I think I'm going to live in these.

Diggin' my I Dream of Jeannie Pants! I just got them, so too soon to tell for the long term, but right off the bat: the material and fit is super cute, comfortable, and flattering. (For reference- I tend to be a size 8/medium and am about 5'4" tall and I got the 2-10 size) The material does seem a bit cheap so I can see how they would tear like in other reviews. Good thing I am not a gymnast or flexible because I could see them tearing right open if you did the splits. Would buy again if they keep the price low/affordable since they are obviously not meant for long term use. Will likely launder in a bag and on the gentle cycle in attempt to keep them in tact for awhile (fingers crossed!) Update: even being very careful with washing- got a tear right after the first use. Such a bummer since they seemed great otherwise. Would only buy again if priced under $20. Wouldn't pay more since you cannot guarantee more than one use.

Woven Material Means No Stretch I usually would wear a 12 in modern women’s pants sizes because everything has some stretch to it. Keep in mind these pants do not stretch at all. I am 5’1”, 176 lbs with the weight in the lower half and I am pretty much at capacity for the seat/inseam (not that the seams are stressed, more that the pants will start to crease strangely in the front - if I could do the splits, I’d be able to do them easily and without an incident). For curvy girls - these only need minor adjustment for great fit Due to my shape, I struggle a great deal to find pants that fit. Usually my only options are granny styles or looks that are too risque for work. I checked the size chart and ordered a 12 to fit my hips, knowing it would be big in the waist. Of course, the stats help when reading reviews, so: 29.5" waist and 45" hips, 5'5" tall and 160 lbs. You can see in the first photo how loose the waist was. A quick adjustment with my sewing machine fixed the fit, and overall I'm pleased with how these pants look. Eventually I may have a go at letting down the hem to get a little more length out of them. For us ladies with significant curves, these pants require very minimal tailoring to achieve a lovely fit. At this price point, I'm thrilled to get such a professional look. One star off because they're extremely plastic feeling inside (though not outside). Overall, I'd highly recommend for curvy girls with basic sewing skills.

Curvy girl approved! These slacks are curvy girl approved. I usually wear a size 12. I ordered a 14 because you just never know when shopping online. I wish I had ordered the 12. These fit pretty true to size. They have a good amount of stretch in them as well so you could probably even go down a size if you like a tighter fit. They have a pretty high rise so no butt cracks will be on display. There are no belt loops so if you’re like me and order them too big, you will likely be pulling them up through-out the day. The pockets are partially sewn down but can be accessed if desired. The pants are a tad short. I’m 5’6 I like them. I will probably return for a smaller size.

Soft and cozy but size up I love these pajamas! They are cute, cozy, and warm. I’m typically a women’s small (5’6” 130Ibs) but I like my pajamas a little loose so I ordered a medium and large. I ordered from the same seller and they even arrived in the same bag but they were made with different fabric and were wildly different in size. The large pants fit me like a glove and the top was loose but not overly so. I pulled the pants up because I like a high waisted fit and they still sit at my ankles. The fabric is stretchy and very plush. I was surprised they looked so similar to the stock photos. The buttons are on tight and there were only a few loose threads. I wish the arms were a little longer so the elastic could sit at my wrist instead of my arm but it’s still comfy. The inside of the pockets is a little scratchy and I’d prefer fabric covering the elastic on the ankle and wrist ruffles. Overall, the large pajamas were 5 stars. The medium pajama pants were too small to get on and too short. The top fit fine but the fabric on both is rougher, cheaper and less plush feeling. I would not have kept the medium even if they weren’t too small.

Incredible, but sadly too short... Firstly, I'm 5'3 130lbs, and ordered these in small. Everything about them, other than length, is incredible!! They feel luxurious on and the elastic waistband makes them feel very comfortable. The color is the most perfect light beige.The only reason for the 3 stars is the length. Great quality This dress pant is nice. Great office pants or if you’re going to an event and have to dress business casual. The fabric is smooth and feels soft. I got the solid beige and its true color. It’s not see through. Fabric is a nice thicker quality. The detail in the front is different, but in a nice way. True to size. Comfortable and nice quality pants.

Great pants - you need these!! Now I got these beautiful pants to wear to work but you can definitely play with this look. You can put a crop top on with it and some sneakers and it’s a look. I got a large and the waist was perfect . The pleats gave me thigh room without looking baggy. The color is rich and can go with any color. I’m a color girl so these pants give me plenty to play with. The fabric is not heavy. But I did wear them in 30 degree weather in Michigan…. I wasn’t outside long, my car to the door but they were fine. I don’t think these are tall girl friendly, they hit me perfect and I am 5’1.

Perfect pants for weight fluctuations. I was injured in a car accident and gained quite a bit of weight. With that being said, I need to order new pants that would fit my new sizes. I saw these and remembered I had similar pants of a smaller size. I’m a fan of sticking to what I know. I am 5’6” tall. My waist is 31 inches and my hip is 44 inches. According to the size chart, I needed a large. The pants fit true to size. The pants themselves feel like really good quality. They’re lightweight but the material is still heavy. It’s not flimsy or see-through. These pants feel super durable. They’re comfortable yet still give a very professional appearance due to the style and design. Most people hate the scrunchie waist but I love it because it gives room for me to lose weight without having to have the pants tailored. The length of the pants is regular length, not long or short. My one complaint is the buttons. They’re hideous. That’s not a major issue for me because I can just change them.

Quality Made, Lined But To Big On Me I'm not one to wear satin pants but these csught my eye. Since these don't stretch I got the large going by my waist size but should have gotten the medium. I thought since these were cropped they would be full length on 5'1" me & with taller shoes they wouldn't drag on the floor. They drag. They are so soft & comfortable & made so well with the nice lining sewed in. The front lies flat, has belt hoops & the pants have a front zipper. I really wish they weren't so overly big on me because I wanted to wear these to be dressy comfortable but they will go unused. They feel so smooth like pajamas but you can't see through them thanks to the lining.

Good for the price I got this pair first, and then also the white one. First you see what they look like with straight bottom. They become longer after being ironed, out of the package. I'm 5'10", so it is an acceptable length. However, It's still a little short for me, when I walk and move around and I found them not very flattering. I had this idea. Purchased a pair of shoelaces in similar color; cut a small hole on the 'wrong' side of the bottom of each pant leg so that it is between the very bottom and the horizontal seam, and then I threaded a shoelace in each, to tie them around the ankle. And these pants become what you see on the last picture, with a tie on the outer side of the leg. This style is more attractive to me, and works better with this garment for my height. I did the same with the white pair. And I wear both frequently. I haven't had a chance to wash them yet, since I wear them gently and mostly for walks. I heard from other reviews that these pants shrink and pill. Shrinkage is the bigger worry. I have other linen pants that shrink and stretch with washes and wear. My plan with THESE pants is to iron them while wet after the wash, and stretch them out, then hang-dry. In my experience, this works with clothes that are prone to shrinking. At least for a while. For pilling/fuzz, I keep a fabric-shaving machine. It's been a great help for many years, and there's a great selection of them right here on Amazon. Hope this helps.

Attractive belt but concerned about longevity of material-Part leather part PU-brown side is dark I am around 120 pounds and 5'6 and usually wear 0-2. I ordered a size small and there are several holes still available and it fits great. However, I am concerned about the longevity of the material. It is not as high quality as I expect from Calvin Klein. This is made of split leather and PU leather. In concept, the PU leather coating would make the split leather a little more durable. However, the belt began to show signs of wear after trying it on. The metal bracket of the belt is coated and I can see the joining seams. It still has a nice appearance. The brown side is very dark and almost black in appearance. I am not confident in the longevity of this belt. I still like the belt but have not had it long and hesitate to recommend it fully.

Pretty standard belt I am normally a size 12/14 and went with the Large, but I should have gotten an XL. Around my waist, the belt is at the last notch and around my upper body (think, sinching at the waist dresses, etc), it fits me better. I guess that’s good for the most part as I won’t have any “extra overhang” of the belt. (But, of course, that means I can’t gain any weight either, haha). For reference, I am 5’4, 185lbs, and apple shaped. Should have definitely sized up. I love that the belt is reversable. I did notice the white size has a CK design stamped across it, but it’s not terrible as you can’t really see it and it just makes the white side look textured. The material is durable, and I think this belt will hold up for a long time.

Tall Girls Dream! Will stretch. Update: have owned for a couple of months now and they def do stretch out. I got the 8 and they were a touch whisker like on the crotch area but after a couple of wears, they stretch out to fit perfect, just like my navy ones did.i already owned these slacks in Navy that I purchased at Lohmans a bit ago. I'm 5'10 and a size 8. And the navy ones fit perfect and needed no tailoring. My old black slacks got lost so I purchased these in black and I hoped they'd fit like the Navy ones. If you are used to teen type sizes like H&M or Forever 21 than yes go down a size but if you are used to woman's brands like CK, Kenneth Cole, or Jones New York, than stay to your true size. At first I was nervous because they were a little tighter in the thighs than my navy ones but I wore them around the house for a day and they stretched to fit perfectly. These slacks do have lots of stretch which is great for comfort but know that they will give slightly once you're wearing them so a little tight when you first wear them is good. Otherwise they'll be baggy and nobody wants saggy slacks butt. They are long. The cut is perfect for women with a little hip. Not boot cut but wide enough at the bottom to give you a little balance. You can see in my photo that at 5'10 they go all the way to floor and at 39in hips fit pretty perfect. There's a little pull in photo from way I was standing but that's it. Good luck!

Fashionable denim cargo pants I really enjoy these cargo pants. They have a very sturdy feel without being bulky or heavy. These cargo pants can be worn perfectly high-waisted, but also don't hug my lower belly too much that it's unflattering.

Lightweight Linen Pants The Linen Capri Pants for Women Casual 2024 Summer Drawstring Elastic High Waist Capris Wide Leg Cropped Trousers are true linen pants. While they are thin they are not seethrough. They are lightweight, have an elastic high waist with a working drawstring, and are a great wardrobe addition for the summer months. I am 5'3" and very curvy, I ordered a size large to fit comfortably over my hips. These pants fit nicely, they offer plenty of room and comfort, the length hits about mid-calf and they have a nice flare. The loose flowy fit makes them perfect for hot summer days. The waistband is attractive and a shirt/blouse could be worn tucked or untucked. I look forward to wearing these capris throughout the summer and am very happy I made the purchase.

You can trust the size chart in the photos. I am 5’6”, 123 lbs, and normally a size 4 in pants. I chose the medium based off the waist measurement on the size chart and I’m glad I did. The waist has elastic so there is some give if you are between sizes. The material is not stretchy so I would recommend double-checking the hip measurements before ordering. The legs are wide and full length on me. I ordered the “khaki” which is actually very light yellow with no tan tones. The material is thin but not as revealing as expected during wear. I would prefer a darker color so I wouldn’t have to worry about under things as much. My review photos were taken after my initial try-on after removing the pants from the jar. I had not washed nor ironed but wanted to get pictures posted to show the thinness of the khaki aka light yellow. I like the fit and feel of these cotton summer pants. It’s nice to have a loose and breathable material for the Texas summers. Like most linen pants, these wrinkle easily. The color is my biggest gripe but that’s personal preference.

Comfy and lightweight pants These are nice lightweight trousers. There is some stretch in the waist for some forgiveness, and the wide leg doesn't hug too tight on my hips. My only disappointment is that they're a little of an awkward length on me (I'm 5' 7"). They're not short enough to feel like crops, and they're not long enough to be normal length. Kind of a high water look? I think paired with some wedges they'll look better on. But otherwise the fit is what I hoped for and is comfortable. Would recommend.

I ended up going with the Orange pants in XL. At the time, I was about 210 lbs and I am 5'5".These are nice pants. They are a bit thin, so when I wore them, I put on a boy short underneath to smooth out the look. The crotch area is a bit high for my taste. So, even though they fit great, the crotch area can look a bit tight, giving the perception that they are too small, which was not the case. With a slight heel, I was great to go without having to hem them.I felt the Orange color 'popped' better over the Red, but both are nice colors.

Comfy…but for work. Size reference: I am 5’6” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered these pants in size small. The fit is true to size in the waist. The back is elastic so there is a little give if you are between sizes. When standing, the pants are full length but when I sit the cuffs come up to mid-shin. Description: I love the design with the pleating. I can get by with pairing with heels to wear to work because the ankles don’t have the bunched elastic jogger look. I also like the real button, zipper fly, and belt loops for the same reason. The outside material is soft, like yoga pants. The inside is lined with slick polyester. There is some stretch but not like true sweat pants. I do worry that the pants will snag easily because of the material. Also, I hope the rayon doesn’t shrink if I accidentally dry these. Recommendation: I love these pants. They are comfy like loungewear but dressed up enough for business casual. I ordered the green and there is some heathering in the background. If not for that, I would probably go ahead and order the black but heathered black looks too faded to pass as work pants. This is the 8th pair of pants I’ve gotten lately and by far the most comfy! I highly recommend!!!!

Good Quality Black Pants - Order According to Seller Chart Dimensions So I'm usually a size small but since I'm 5'9", I usually have to order larger sizes so the pant leg will be long enough to fit in the ankle area, and not short. So I ordered a large to ensure the pant leg would be long enough but the pants are too baggy in the back. Their size chart is correct, which is why I'm not deducting a star, so if I would've gone according to their chart dimensions/sizing, seems the small may have been better. I just don't like a tight waisted pant which is another reason I didn't order the small. But overall the pant quality is good for a work environment, paired with a nice blouse, even a blazer. The stitch and hems are good and the material is thick. They also feel like they're easy to wash and durable to last a good while. I highly recommend these pants. Just order the right size.

I Like These Pants I've been looking for pants like these for quite awhile. I wanted lightweight pull-on pants with pockets big enough for a cell phone that I can wear during the summer months. For me, the hardest part was determining the right size. The online size chart indicated that I should wear a size 18P, which is larger than anything I own. Nevertheless, I bought a pair and they turned out to be too big. I knew as soon as I tried them on, though, that I wanted one in every color. So, I ordered two more pairs in both a 16P and a 14P. The 16P turned out to be the best fit, even though it is still a little loose; but I definitely didn't want pants that were too tight. These are good every-day pants for a retired woman in her 70's. I am 5'2" and weigh about 160. I had no problems with making multiple returns.

Super soft but has some drawbacks These are really soft, priced reasonably, and very comfortable. Good points: - sizing is pretty accurate for the chart (I'm usually a L and the chart suggested XXL which was good) - color is beautiful - fabric is lightweight and super soft but it does show every dimple and buldge - The expenditure is totally on the mark for the value. Draw backs: - the waist is so high! A good 4" above my belly button. - the top seam is tightened with elastic so you can slide it down your waist without it creeping up - it has these faux pockets, so they just add extra bulk to the belly area and serve no purpose - it came wrapped in not one but three single use plastic bags for no good reason - it has a strong chemical smell, likely due to be wrapped up in so much plastic that needs a few times washing to get out - they are little long for my 5' 5" self so I have to hem them by a good inch or so Overall, these are cute and very cozy. I love the color too. I'd recommend them but be aware of the above issues before you buy.

Heavy wide leg pants What's good - • Heavy material gives it a high quality, flowy & elegant feel • Pockets are always great to have • Elastic waistband is comfortable and gives the pants a totally relaxed fit However... • length of S-sized pants still too long on a 5'2" frame (since it's supposed to be ankle length) • the front pleats are unnecessary and create a puffy look • the fitting on the hips is a bit too wide and crotch area is too low - looks unflatteringly baggy

Might be ok if you’re 5’5 or under If I had the right body type for these pants, I wouldn’t hesitate to give them 5 stars because they are really well made, the fabric is heavy and looks expressive. Unfortunately they don’t work for me. First of all, the length - I’m 5’7 and these pants are just way too short. They don’t work as ankle pants because of the wide leg, and I would need at least 3 extra inches of length in order to be able to wear them. Sizing- I got a Large for my 33” waist, 43” hip. You can tell the size is not right for me by the way they balloon on the front in the stomach area. I should have gotten a Medium. Keep in mind that the waist has an elastic so the waist stretches a good bit and the fabric itself is stretchy so my hips definitely would have been ok in a Medium. My suggestion is that if you’re 5’5 or under, give these pants a chance and if you’re in between two sizes, go with the smaller size.

Great Linen-like Material The material of this outfit was linen-like and had a good weight to it. The fit for me was a bit off. I am usually a large bottom and medium top. The bottoms were a bit tighter than expected and the top vest was too big and boxy. The pants are high waisted and wide leg. The material was stiffer so flattering and didn't cling. There was a zipper and elastic in the waistband so they could be put on easily. For me I would need to alter the vest for it to look better on but I could wear the pants with another top. The pants were a great length on my 5'4" frame when worn higher on my waist. The pants hit right above the floor so can be worn with flat sandals.

Perfect fit These pants are great. There's a lining like shorts inside and it doesn't ride up. You notice it a little bit when you're walking, like the hem of the shorts, but it's not very noticeable. It might be more visible if you're pretty dark skinned, though. They're flowy and the wrinkles steamed out easily--however, they wrinkle when you're sitting for a bit (I was at my desk half of the day) and those new wrinkles didn't go away when you get up. Not a deal breaker for me, but worth noting. I love that the waist fit me perfectly. There's no elastic, no stretch, and I used the measurements in the at size chart. These will prob be too long if you're short. I'm 5'8" and had on 2.5" heels and they were just above the floor maybe an inch. For size reference, I ordered XL and it's perfect. I'm 5'8", 173#, about a size 10. My mid waist is 34 and my hips are 40 inches.

Cute pants with some flaws I was really excited for these pants and love the way they look online. In person I was disappointed a little with some aspects. For reference I am 5‘,5“ tall and 115 pounds. My measurements are 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips. I ordered the size extra small and it fit perfectly. It was even slightly snug in the waist and hips on me, but not a dealbreaker. I was disappointed online the color is a very pretty light sky blue. However in person it’s a darker, dusty blue color. I already have pants this color and really wanted a light pale, blue pant. The other thing I didn’t care for are the front pleats they’re not very flattering and add a little bit of bulk to me. Finally they were also a little bit short. I’m only 5‘5“ tall and these fell right at my ankles to where I really need to wear flats with them in order for them to not look like high waters. I typically like my pants longer, and to cover most of my shoe. All in all they are an OK pair of pants. I wasn’t thrilled at the color in person is not the same and didn’t particularly like the front pleats but I will still wear them. Hope that helps!

They're soft on the inside and nice and long -- I'm almost 6 feet tall and these fit perfectly. These aren't super warm so just be careful. You could run in them in like 35 or 40 degree weather probably, but they wouldn't be warm enough to walk in temps like that. But that's not why I bought them and not what they're intended for. Buy the fleece lined leggings if you want something very warm for going outside in the cold. (Or just buy them because they're super comfy and cute too!)I really like this brand and own a lot of clothing of theirs now (like 15 pairs of leggings, 4 tops, some shorts etc). I wanted these to be a little loose honestly so I went up a size. All of my other pants are Larges but I bought an XL and like it. Great Idea in Theory; Not Cute (On Me) in Reality 🤪 I really wanted to like these pants! I mean, they look SO cute on the skinny model!! 😋 But, on me -- they just look frumpy and make my body look bigger than it actually is. 😝 Unfortunately, these slacks just didn't hug me in the right places. For one, I'm almost always bloated and this waistband was WAY too tight, as in -- tight enough where I cut a slit on each side of the waistband so I didn't feel like a stuffed potato. 😂 For reference, I'm 5'2, weigh <125 lbs., wear a DDD bra size, and have defined, slender legs. These pants accentuated my belly, made my booty look flatter somehow (still not quite sure why....?), and didn't do anything to help my legs stand out...at least in a positive way! 😝 Although very comfy and flowy, I feel like these pants made my legs look bigger than they are. In addition, they were just a tad little too long while wearing flats, and they didn't seem to look right with the two different types of heels I tried them on with. While I will say the material is quite soft and comfortable, I wouldn't rely on these pants to keep me super warm when I'm feeling really cold. They'd be best suited to wear on days when there's a light breeze. Again, while I really wanted to like these pants, unfortunately, they're not a home run for me. But, if you're rather slender with a small tummy, you might love them. ✌️

The good, the bad and the rest Well... kinda on the fence... I went with the Size: X-Large | Color: Khaki. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. These pants have a lot of orange/pink to them for being "khaki" depending on the lighting. But we all know khaki is one of those colors we all have different ideas of what it actually is. This fabric is very light weight, flowy, with some small stretch and they kinda look like a linen. They are 100% polyester though. That means they haven't shrunk or anything in the wash and they don't hold the wrinkles nearly as badly as linen does. But that also means they don't breath as well either. On the softness scale of 1-5, 5 being super soft, these fall around a 4. They are so light that they aren't often in contact with your skin unless you're sitting, but the texture has not bothered my sensitive skin at all. The stitching all seems fine, decent construction. The pockets are a nice feature. Maybe it's because of the size, but the front and back are not that different. And on me, it's unfortunate where some of the pleats fall from the elastic waist. It adds to the look of my belly. Not something I need exaggerated. With a shirt over it, it can look a little better but I will probably alter the waist line in time. Overall, they are pretty decent pants that would work in an office or for traveling. With the price being under $20, that bumps these an extra star. I would say they are worth checking out. Peace and comfy pants

I like how it has belt loops and how it would appear after washing with no changes. I appreciate how broad the legs are. It's soft and smooth. I appreciate how it has two open front pockets, but it would be wonderful to have two open back pockets.

So nice but a bit larger than expected In retrospect I should have ordered a size 4 instead of 6. I’m 5’-5” and 120 lbs but I don’t have a tiny waist so I assumed that these would be too tight in a 4. Wrong. Size 6 is pretty big yet super comfortable and they look cool. I’m ordering a size 4 in another color.

Good quality but not long enough for tall people These pants are super cute, soft, comfy, and good quality. However, I will be returning because they are very high watered on me, and when I sit, the end of the pant leg sits at mid calf on me. For reference, I’m 5’ 9” and ordered a size 4. Very nice pants for Fall/Winter These are such nice, corduroy pants! I got them for my granddaughter, age 15, who really liked the look of them on Amazon. She’s wearing an adult size 8-10, and is very picky about what she likes to wear for school. Meaning, they had to be black. Haha. Seriously, though, black is her signature color, as she proudly proclaims. She loves that these are cargo pants, and also that they’re corduroy, rather than plain old denim. The corduroy is thin-wale, and the fabric is very soft, making it a perfect, cozy feel for Fall/Winter. The pants fit her perfectly, too. The fabric has a nice bit of stretch in the waist, but not obviously so - it’s just enough to stretch over the hips, then fit her perfectly in the waist. The length is even great! So many pants are too long on even average-size women/girls. These are a tiny bit long on my 5’4” granddaughter, but she prefers them to be long and slouchy. She’s a tough sell these days, but she is honestly delighted with these pants. The price of $34.99, as of this writing, is very reasonable for a quality-made pair of pants. These are machine wash and dry, perfect for a busy family with 3 kids. I’m very pleased with these pants.

THESE WILL SHRINK AND BREAK YOUR HEART I ADORED these pants when they first arrived. I’m 5ft 4.5in tall, 185 lbs, and ordered the XL. They were the perfect, breezy, floor-length pants of my dreams. They skimmed my problem area (my postpartum belly) gracefully and made my stomach look flatter. I washed them once on the cold delicate cycle and hung them to air dry and they shrank at least 2 or 3 inches. They used to touch the top of my feet, and now they are above my ankles. I’m not sure if I’ll wear them at all now - instead of lengthening my proportions, they make my legs look shorter and stubbier. Extremely disappointing and a waste of money. Save yourself the heartache! Unless you’re exactly 5’2, in which case they’d probably end up being perfect lol. Ladies, know your fabrics. Anything 100% rayon or viscose will shrink. Look for polyester options instead. Check my other reviews if you’d like to see the brand I found that worked way better for me.

Not flattering if you are curvy!! I’m so disappointed. These are good quality, nice material and well made. Nice fit, silky soft, and oh so comfy! These Baggy Wide Leg Pants are certainly wide and baggy, but it makes them extra comfortable to wear. The material is sort of heavy in weight and yet it is not thick. I think they will be of medium warmth, not too warm, but enough for chilly fall or spring days. They are very soft inside and outside. The fit is great and flowing. Legs are a bit long on me with my 5'4" height, but once I used the ties at the ankle to tighten them somewhat, being a little long was not a problem anymore. They have two full sized pockets. They are well made. I love them and would definitely like to have more of them to wear! I can and do recommend these for the value, comfort, and fitting true to size.

Nice pants; a little too short for me I requested these pants on the chance that they are actually longer than the size chart indicates... but nope, the chart is spot on. I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs and I'm wearing a size Large; a Medium would have fit but I was going for baggy and long, which, together, are on-trend for FW24. They are really nice pants -- nice medium weight, full in all of the right places and the cargo pockets add a funky twist. These pants are a very nice length for someone of average height, but even positioned below my waist, they fall too short to be cute on me. They do have a half inch seam so I'll let them out. I think these pants are a great value -- really, really nice.

Soft & Comfy, Pricey, But Not A Great Fit For Pear-Shaped Women, I *WILL* Luncle Dry! I'm just under 5'8" and down to 195lbs at the time of my order. I lost over 60 lbs last year, from a size 22-24 to I'm not sure what size exactly. This might be important for others to know. I'm still losing slowly, but based on the measurements I took of myself before ordering (Waist 39", Hips 45", Inseam 32-34"), I optimistically ordered size 14 -- and miraculously, they fit, just a bit snug but not overly tight. I think the slight stretch to the material helped with that. The tag was so amusing that I had to share. I'm too old to deal with hand washing anything, so if it doesn't survive my machine, it's not worth having. You can bet that not only did I wash it in my machine (cold water, normal setting, with the rest of my clothes), I also tumbled it dry -- to be fair, I did follow the instructions to not luncle dry it! They came out just fine, with no noticeable shrinkage. If machine washing & drying makes them wear out faster, that's just too bad. The most I'll go out of my way for something special I rarely wear is to put it in a net bag to wash and hang to dry -- but that's only if I have the time and inclination. :) The only problem I have with them is the same as with many pants: there is a gap at my lower back since my hips & butt are bigger than my waist. I have to wear a belt with these pants to correct that. They are not really made for women with my type of curves. I'm also a notorious penny-pincher, and these pants are nearly $40, which is almost twice as much as I prefer to pay for pants. These are comfy & soft, but the material is also thin, so I don't know how long they'll last before they tear. I've only worn them a few times since I received & washed them. For this price, I hope they last a long time. If they do tear or have any problems prematurely, I will come back to update.

BEST PANT EVER. Super stretchy! Stretches out over time - good to size down I bought these first in white in a 6, and LOVED THEM, then they became baggy as I wore them (in a cute way though). So I bought the grey and red in a 4 and they fit perfect. I normally go between a 4-6 in normal pants so I'd recommend sizing down because of the stretch. These are my new favorite pants. I have worn them the last 4 times I have gone out. Perfect for any occasion, and great with a bodysuit. I have a small waist and wider hips and the 4's fit perfectly, no belt needed.

So comfortable! I got these for weekend hikes and Girl Scout field trips with my family but will also probably wearing them for much more than that as they are so cute and comfortable. I will now be buying a pair for my daughter as she loves them too. The material is very soft but incredibly sturdy. Definitely a quality pair of pants. I like that they came in a variety of colors and the side pockets take me back to my Junior high school days! I am 5'6 152lbs and generally wear a sz6 pants and these were slightly loose on my waist but very comfortable so I don't mind.

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

This will be the most HONEST REVIEW you've ever seen..... I bought the Black in size 12 and the Green in size 14, but only bcuz a Green size 12 didn't become available Until AFTER I let the seller know they were running out of sizes. However, I have tried Shrinking the Green size 14 and they did shrink a little bit. I will try to Shrink the Green some more, but I will need to Stretch the Legs while they are hot and/or Use a Steamer to Stretch the Length, bcuz I really don't want the Length to Shrink! Also, as you will see in the photos, the Black pair had Tags etc strung into a little black loop inside the back of the pants, whereas the Green pair didn't have any Tags nor a special little black loop for the Tags, so I almost kinda feel that the Green pair were a Return, but that's ok, bcuz I've since washed them... But truthfully, I'm shocked that these pants/jeans are as Amazing, Soft and Super Comfy as they are! When I first grabbed the package from being downstairs and outside at the door, it didn't feel like a package of jeans. It felt waaaaay too light for jeans and I was almost disappointed! But... I opened the package and decided to try them on anyway! Wow!! I am absolutely blown away! Not only do they Fit according to the Size Chart, (I'm 5' - 6.5" and about 145ish pounds) but they are a really nice, soft and a "comfy around the waist" pair of jeans, that I honestly think will become my Favorite pair of jeans!! Due to stomach issues, I don't usually like anything around or touching my upper tummy, but these are sooo Light and Soft that they honestly Don't bother my tummy 1 bit! I'm even been sitting and laying on the couch to check out their comfortness and they pass with flying colors.. COMFORT Beyond Belief!! And bcuz of how extremely Comfortable they are, I am now going to order a couple more pairs!I've already ordered and received the Green pair, but I'm going to get another Black pair like these ones, and I think I'll get a pair of the Darker Blue ones IF the seller has more of the Blue ones in a size 12?!? I do have 1 "Slight" issue, and it's nothing major, it's a "Me Thing" just bcuz I'm very picky when it comes to a Pants Length, I do prefer my pants to be Longer, and so, I wish these jeans were another Inch, or even preferably 2 inches Longer!! But as they are right now, they almost touch the floor at my 5' - 6.5" of height! And like I said, it's a "Me Thing", I just prefer Extra Long Pants!! However, after washing them and before hanging them up to dry, I'm going to try and Stretch the Legs out in order to Gain a little bit more Length!! And IF this doesn't work, I may have to use Hot Steam to Stretch them out a bit, bcuz Hot Steam always works when wanting to Stretch out clothing!! **I have since washed both pairs in cool water and tumbled dried them for a bit with a little bit of Heat and I was able to stretch the Length back.** Anyway, being an extremely Picky person, I personally and highly Recommend these jeans! You Won't be Disappointed!! I truly hope you Grow to Love them as much as I do, and I've only had them on for 30 minutes just to try them out... (I've now had them for 2 days and have washed both the Black and Green pair and All was good) But I do know already, that they will and have, become my FAVORITE pair of Cargo jeans... And yes, it's true that they are Not a Heavy Cotton jean, but that's why I Love them so much, bcuz with them being a lighter fabric, it makes them Easier to move around in! I can do Squats and whatever else in them, plus Sit for Hours in them and I'm still very comfortable and it's all bcuz of how Flexible they are with being a Lighter fabric/material... --Bottom Line, I have absolutely NO PROBLEM with these Cargo jeans! They are absolutely Amazing and I'm sure you will Love them as much as I do...--

Not Sure About These... I am 5'4", rather athletic build, and not much of a booty.😭 I hover between 145-150lbs. I bought a medium, which was what was suggested. I wear anything from a size 6 to a 10 in bottoms, although 8 is my overall average. These are VERY high- waist on me, but I have a short torso so that might be why. They aren't as loose fitting at I thought they would be, but they aren't tight. The waistband is very stretchy so don't panick when you first pull them out the bag. I thought they were child's pants at first! I bought the army green color and it is much darker than what is usually considered army green. It's almost like what would be called a "forest green". The pockets are fine although they land lower on my butt than I would like. Note that the little front patch is just that--a patch of material that is sewn on, not an actual pocket. The material is similar to a lightweight chino or khakis, good for fall and spring. With some leggings underneath you could wear them in the winter as well. Overall, they are very comfortable, I just am not sure how to style them. They aren't tight at all, so if you like how they look on me, get your regular size, but double check the size chart measurements.

The front pockets are a good size, while the back pockets are just for the look. The legs are really wide and the length was good for my 5'7" height as they did not drag on the ground. The only issue I had with these pants is the waist seems to run a bit small. I normally wear an XL and these were slightly uncomfortable to wear to work. There is some elastic at the back, but I intend to move the clasp a bit to make them more comfortable. If you are between sizes, I recommend sizing up. They are pricey when compared to similar products as I am not familiar with this brand.

These pants are very similar to a higher priced name brand business (more formal professional) wear pants I have from decades ago. So very pleased. Different Fit For Different Colors! I am 5'9" and a pretty skinny 126 lbs. I bought 2 pairs in the exact same size - Red, Brown, Medium. I returned the red. They did not fit the same neither were they both of consistent quality. The red fit smaller & were narrower/tapered in the legs. The material was poly/cotton & thinner. The brown was a great fit, long enough and thick, comfy material. They are 65% Rayon, 35% Spandex! That means they will never shrink in the dryer. I don't detect any stretching from the Spandex content, which is odd, but for me, not a bad thing. Haven't washed them yet, so we'll see how they hold up otherwise. Maybe these pants were made in different countries, but now I'm looking to get a different color that is exactly the same as the brown pair. If you have bought other colors & you see Rayon/Spandex mix, post it in your review. I'll def order that color. It seems that I can't post 2 different reviews, Amazon keeps giving me the first review to edit for the second pair. So I've combined both reviews here. 2 stars for the Red, 4 stars for the brown .... Guess I'll average to 4 stars so the browns don't reflect badly.

Exceeded expectation, love the fit Size reference: I am 5'6" and 129 lbs, normally a size 4 in pants/jeans. The size chart suggested a small but I wanted a loose waist so I went with a medium. I am pleased with the up-size! The legs are full length, the waist is not digging into me but doesn't look too big. Description: I have ordered many wide leg pants so I expected the fabric to be chiffon type and flowy. However, the material is firm polyester, like a blazer with some stiffness and shape. Not thin and not see through. I love the pleats on the waistline as it does a lot flatter the under-navel region. They stick out a bit which helps camouflage pooches and sharp hip bones. The hip pockets are big enough to be functional. The waistline is extra-high, which I like from the front. There is something weird with the back view, perhaps the faux pockets are in the wrong place proportionally. As for quality of construction, the pants are well made but not name brand dupe worthy. I initially ordered strictly as work pants. However, after trying them on I think they would be cute with a crop tank and new sneakers for the weekend as well. Recommendation: I love them. I like the way the feel, look, and fit. I've already added them to my watch list and will order the navy if they go on sale. The pale pink is perfect for the spring. At the current price of $29.99, these pants exceeded expectations.

I actually like these & they're incredibly comfortable - review from a fluffy mom I am reviewing this as a mom with a bit of a fluffy body (I am EXTREMELY self-conscious about my waist and belly so no photos of the waistband on), 5'5"ish and usually wear an 8/10 -- so definitely not young and cute like the model. I ordered a Large in these pants and I am very pleasantly surprised.First, they are a cotton-twilly material that doesn't feel particularly soft initially but actually moves pretty well when wearing the pants. They have a boxy feel -- but that is typical of the cargo look (and my lack of an actual waist). I give the waist band 100 stars -- it is a mix of elastic and button/zipper so it has some give, doesn't compress on my belly or slip below a muffin top and it and is really super comfortable. I won't show any photos, but the band comes right above my belly button and securely above my hips and covers the small of my back completely-- so they cover everything and sit in a very comfortable spot. Even when I sit they are very comfortable and stay in place above my belly and hips. There are belt loops too just in case you need them, but waist wise the size is perfect for me, esp with the elastic!Length wise, these seem to run a bit long for me -- however I actually really find the rolled-up style with the sandals cute. For tennis shoes I rolled the hem up once and I think they are okay. The boots photos have no roll. The sandals photos took 3 full wide rolls on the hem. These could easily be hemmed, but I often like my pants on the longer side so I can wear them with different types of shoes and in different season -- so I am good with these. But, yes, I would say they run long.Lots and lots of pockets -- 2 back pockets, 2 hip pockets, and 4 cargo pockets. The cargo pockets are real and not just decoration. The top ones just have a flap, the bottom ones have a button with the flap. So lots of storage if you want it.I actually surprised myself and like these. I am always nervous about ordering online because I never feel anything fits me in a flattering way, and I second guessed my order a million times. But now that I have them on and see how incredibly comfortable they are on even with my body style, I am really glad that I pushed order. These are a keeper and I would definitely recommend.

Nice fabric, nicely tailored. Good fit. I'm 5'3" tall, weight 150 pounds. This is a size medium. Plusses: They have the tailoring of the 1940s, with snug fit over hips and waist and flaring to the legs. The elastic on the waistband is wide and quite stretchable to get over hips to put on. Pockets are well made and have the placket to carry tons of stuff. The buttons are 5/8" across giving a nice decorative look. The pants are long for taller people, yet you can tie the legs to lift the hem above your shoes. Minuses: Wish the pockets were velcro closed as the buttons are hard to get through the buttonholes. And when unbuttoned, you can't see them at all. Strings on pant legs are long and could trip up the wearer unless double tied. Nice pair of cargo pants and will be my go-to for casual wear.

Not For Petites. Man, I was so excited for these ETOSELL Baggy Jeans, but sadly they are just not made for us Petites! I am 5'0 & 115 lbs (with a 28" waist & 40" hips) and usually wear a size 6, so that is what I ordered. These here jeans are just way too long for my short self and are also kind of too big everywhere else. I really loved the look of these jeans and even own another pair of ETOSELL Cargo Jeans in a size 6, which fit me really well, so yeah I was definitely disappointed that the fit of these were so off compared to my other pair from this brand. The jeans are made well, they are heavy, do not have stretch to them, but are comfortable. I would not recommend these jeans if you are on the shorter side like myself. I would possibly consider sizing down since these are so baggy? Maybe they would have fit a little better if I got a size 4, not totally sure though...just a thought!

The good, the bad and the rest Heavy weight denim used for these so should hold up. I chose the Dark Blue | Size: X-Large. For reference I'm over 50 years old. I also like that the color is uniform. I don't care for the worn out faded in patches look. The inseam is about 29.5". The stitching and construction all seem solid. And the front pockets are nice and deep, can even hold my kindle 7. The crotch fit is a little weird on me, but that's due to my shape. You're experiences will probably be different. In general, these are good jeans. Peace and sturdy pants

Trendy, cozy jeans I'm one of the last of the millennials to switch up from my beloved skinny jeans into something more "trendy" and I honestly spent hours and went from store to store to store in malls and shopping centers to find things, only to be disappointed by the fit, color or the sizing. Amazon was my last effort so I went on a hunt and found these. They are trendy, cozy, and I feel extremely fashionable and relaxed in them all at the same time. I am 5'5" and weigh 121, I'm a Levi's 6/28 and with more of a booty than anything else, so finding pants that fit me well is always hard. Not these! I got a size 06. They're perfect for anything! I can wear a belt or go without, I can do heels or sandals, dress them up or down for any occasion. I'm definitely a fan! The only downside is that they REEK of leather. I washed them with an extra rise on heavy cycle and it still smells heavily of leather. My legs even smell like leather after wearing them. I'm about to throw them into the washer for the second time since Saturday (today is Wednesday) in an attempt to at least dial down the aroma. We'll see how it goes. Regardless of the smell, they're a fabulous pair of pants and definitely worth it. If you're like me and have been looking for something to switch up your style to fit in a bit more without compromising comfort, give them a try!

Great fit, just a little short Ordered these in the coffee color, size 4 for my daughter - she's 5'5" & 112lbs. The material feels nice, soft with a little stretch, and the thickness is just right - not too thin & not too thick. The construction is well done with neat seams, sturdy zipper, and easy buttoning. As far as the fit goes, these were perfect before washing & drying. The length was just right. After washing & drying we both thought they shrank too much lengthwise, and will look weird if she wears these with any type of heeled shoe. So, if you're on the tall side, don't put these in the dryer! Also, the color is solid brown, not the acid washed look in the pics on the product page. Four stars because they're too short & color is misleading.

For reference, I am 5'3", 140-145 lbs and usually wear a size 8. I ordered a medium, which was a perfect fit, except I did hem them about 2". I do like the split leg design more than I expected. They also have a zipper and button, but honestly I just slip them on like leggings and don't even use the zipper. The quality is nice and the fabric does not look cheap. I cannot think of a negative thing to say, I like everything about them.

Pant legs are different lengths! I ordered these pants with the hopes of wearing them on an upcoming Europe trip. Initially they arrived very wrinkled and long so I steamed them a bit to see what they would truly look like on and this did help shorten them a bit as well. I loved how they truly look like they're fully linen. The waist and proportions of size Small comfortably fit my 5'3" frame just right. The biggest issue though was that one pant leg was longer than the other! In trying them on with shoes, it was visibility apparent that one side reached the ground but the other wasn't. I tried adjusting but the difference was still obvious. Laying the pants down, you can see in the photos how the uneven hemline is up to an inch different. Returned as defective and hopefully the replacement will not have this issue!

Not for petites 5’1, 135 lbs, 30” waist. - The medium is quite tight at 28.75” even with the elastic, and the Large would have been too large. - The belt loops stick out and are a little visually jarring. - But the biggest issue is probably the inseam. Usually there’s a little bit of give on the inseam. Nope, the medium was 30.25” and even in 4.5” platform heels, still touched the ground for me. So, these are good if you are 5’6”+ or have high heels.

Love the pants! A little bit "tall" FYI Reviewed by spouse: These are so fun, I cannot wait to wear them! Pants That Are Comfortable Enough To Be Pajamas These pants are so comfortable, you'll have to double to check to make sure you're not wearing pajamas! They are not thin, so they fall nicely, all while stretching just enough for comfortable all day wear. I ordered them in black, and through multiple wash and dry cycles, the color has not faded to the eye. When first removing from the package there was a button indent on the opposite side of the buttons but it came out during washing. The stitching is nice and consistent, and the pants came out of the dryer (after having been left there for several hours) with no wrinkles, which make these great for traveling! The pockets, while not large enough for a cell phone, are really more for show so you avoid a bulge from an item in the pocket. The fit is true to size, and I am 5'9” and they were long enough for me to wear with a pair of shoes with a 2” heel with no problem. I advise ordering these in multiple colors.

Just not quite what I envisioned I'm 5'5, curvy fit, 135ish. Not long legged. I normally wear a 27/6 in jeans ... I sized up to an 8 in this. Nothing was tight but I didn't live the look. I've seen barrell jeans (not tried on) and mom jeans--each a baggy look. I do own the Free People knit jumper and a FP denim oversized set of bibs and like both.
