Good design and fits well but zipper is not smooth at all This review is for: Womens Wide Leg Jeans High Waisted Stretchy Baggy Jeans for Woman Trouser Jeans for Women Trendy Loose Jeans Listed at $41 these wide leg jeans are actually not that wide leg and are very comfortable. They go above my bellybutton and are High waisted style. I like that the material is fairly thick and the color is not washed off easily. I ordered size 4 for a 5 foot for 122 pounds person and these fit perfectly. I did use their size chart. My only issue with these is that the zipper gets caught and it’s not smooth at all like normal zippers. This feels like a very cheap zipper material/ quality for $40 jeans.

Comically wide legs.... So, I knew that these were straight leg pants per the description and the product photos but I was not prepared for them to be wide legged to the point of looking like a costume. Also I ordered per the size chart and these were a bit big. I would have needed to size down. I was in highschool in the 90s and all the skater chicks listening to the sneaker pimps would be envious of these jeans! Pair them with a sheer lace painted-on crop top and you have the apex of mid 90s skater chick fashion! 😂. If that is what you are going for you have found it in spades! I usually wear a us 12 in jeans. 5' 4" about 140 pounds. Ordered the size 14 per the chart. Flat Garment measurements not stretched for the size 14: 16.5" waist 22.5" hip 30.5" inseam 44" top of waist to hem 13.5" button at waist to crotch seam 12.5" leg bottom hem flat....so when you double that measurment for the circumference that's at least 25" around at the ankle for each leg...lol 🤣 These pants also are very stretchy. The measurements are flat measurements without stretching the material.

Good stretch Wide leg jeans are back in style! I love the color and stretch of these jeans. The back pockets are placed in a good location. The zipper and button are durable. I accidentally ordered these jeans two sizes smaller than usual. I normally wear a size 14 jeans. I weigh 215 pounds. When I ordered JB jeans, I always order one size down because of the stretch. My hips are 44” and accidentally ordered two sizes down, a size 10 instead of size 12. I was able to get these jeans on and since I’ve lost over 35 pounds this year, I will save these for my next goal weight, when I will fit into size 12 clothes at 195lbs. The inseam length is rather long and you can see I have on 3” platform shoes to compensate. You could raw cut the hems or actually sew a hem if your inseam is short like me at 29”. These jeans are cute and can’t wait to wear them more.

I can't speak for durability yet but definitely pleased with the look and feel of these so far.

10,000 Stars! Exactly what I wanted, follow the size chart! Update! These absolutely can be cut!! I cut off about three inches so I can wear them with sneakers or flats but they also look cute with heels. A rotary cutter and ruler made it super easy. I ordered them in the black too and they are SO FANTASTIC! They look just like the $100 Abercrombie ones! So happy! Original review: These pants are SO GOOD!!! I am shocked at how perfect these are! They fit perfectly and are so comfortable. They have SOME stretch to them so they are easy to wear for long periods. They will look better with a heel or even sneakers than a bootie, but I was too lazy to change my shoes and socks! :) Some thoughts below: - Follow the size chart for the best fit. I usually wear a womens 6 or 28" waist which can be a small or medium, based on the size chart, I went with the medium and they are SO perfect! I am 5'7" and 135lbs for reference. - It is a faux leather but it does not have that AWFUL fish smell that some do. The material doesn't look super fake either, it looks great. I was SHOCKED. - The inside almost has a lining to it, they are very comfortable and I imagine will keep you nice and warm - I love that it has belt loops and pockets! Neither are super large, but both appreciated. - No butt pockets but I am literally amazed at how good my butt looks in these! I was NOT expecting that! - The ends of the pant legs are unfinished so I THINK you could cut them if they were too long on you. I cannot say for sure as the length is great for me at 5'7" but it appears that you could (see photos) Overall, I am so in love with these pants, I already went back and ordered them in black. I am not kidding when I say I have ordered 10-15 pairs of faux leather pants to find THIS LOOK and I finally found it with these. I am so happy and I cannot recommend them enough!

Thick fabric & perfect length for 5'4"! I'm 5'4" and currently 155 lbs., and the Medium size fits me almost perfectly (a tiny bit snug at the waist, but I'm losing weight so that won't be an issue for long). The elastic waist pulls in enough that I probably could also have worn a Large for a more relaxed fit through the seat and leg. The length is ideal for me, hitting just at the heel with a break at the ankle. That means if you're above average height, these pants may be a bit short for you. There's not much hem to let out. I usually have low expectations for knit and/or stretch pants, but this fabric is thicker than most. No panty lines visible, and the fabric has a pretty low stretch ratio -- it has enough give that you can move comfortably, but it's not clingy. The ribbing is visible, but neutral enough that from a few feet away the pants just look like dress slacks unless you're in super bright light (my photos were taken in direct sun to show detail, but in average room lighting they look darker). Obviously this applies to the black pants I'm reviewing; if you select a different color, texture visibility may vary. The pockets are large enough to fit my entire hand, but because they're set at an angle, I'm not sure I'd trust them with a large device like a smartphone. The only thing about these pants that I don't care for is the drawstring, which is 1) enormous and can't be tucked out of sight inside the waistband because it's so bulky, and 2) superfluous, because the waist is so heavily elasticized. I ended up pulling the drawstring out of mine (one end is untied, so it's easy) so the pants look less like sweatpants. I usually wear shirts untucked, so the holes in the waistband aren't likely to show. Overall, I'm pleased with the quality, and will put these into rotation with my fall casualwear!

Super Warm Work Pants Size Large has a 28-36" elastic waist, 42" hips, 28" inseam, and 12" high rise. The elastic is medium-light, not too firm, feels pretty comfy even at 36". They are definitely wide leg and are a bit excessively baggy on me but at least I can move around easily in them. It's not entirely clear what these pants are made of but the outer layer is a lovely nice quality sweater material that seems likely to be a blend of synthetic and natural materials, and the inner layer is a synthetic microfleece. The color '01 Nude' is pretty similar to the listing photo, a bit darker, they just photograph more yellow. The pockets are pretty roomy. They do show pantylines despite the thickness. The pants are a bit heavy but it has belt loops if you need a belt to keep them up. It's too early in the year to test them but they seem plenty warm to me and will probably be okay down to like a good 10F is what I would guess. They look quite sharp and fashionable but feel like cozy sweatpants, I like them. I would recommend to wash on cold and hang dry, no dryer ever.

Surprised at how nice these are These are not what I would call velvet. It's more of a stretchy weighty velvety material. This fabric will likely hold up to wear better than velvet which is more delicate. The material is surprisingly heavy in a good way, which makes them hang nicely like in a draping kind of manner. The weight also helps to make them appear well made and a good quality. I am 5' 6", usually order a 31 inseam, and the length is to the floor so taller girls it works. I am between a L and XL but usually opt for XL for bottoms as I'm a bit hippy and these have ample room. I'm pretty sure I could do a L and they would just not be as flowing. So I call this ample true to size. I should also mention there are seam pockets and they don't make my hips look bigger. The sides lie flat. I want these over my typical house clothing of leggings because I'm finding the clinginess of leggings bothers me, but I don't want to go all the way to PJ bottoms. These are very nice for the price and I'm especially pleased with the weight. These are so nice you could probably wear them out with a sparkly top such as New Years or Christmas, but for me they will be stylish lounge wear.I'm so happy with them that I'd say it's a favorite item from Amazon. They are that nice. Also may purchase a second and may try a L.

My absolute fav pants!! Plz read if you get the shrink issue, I found a solution!! The shrinkage is real, BUT THEIRS A SOLUTION. love these pants. I own 4, and live in them!! Lightweight Summer Pants These pull on, elastic waist pants are cute and made from a breathable 95% Rayon, 5% Spandex blend. This gives them a touch of stretch for added comfort. The waistband is a total of 2 3/4”, with a little frill at the top. So the elastic is about 2”, with 3/4” of bunched frill at the top for a cute, feminine look. These pants have front slit pockets and fall at about the top of my foot. I am 5’ 2” and 127 lbs for reference, so they would be more of a capri style on some wearers. Cute Summer pants!

These pants are great! I bought them for work (job requires black pants) these are perfect! They fit me really good. I'm 6ft, 155-160. I got the black XL 35" inseam. Look and feel great These pants are a great-quality stretchy material that feels good. As you can see in the close-up picture, it’s got a diagonal pattern more similar to jeans rather than the completely smooth knit I’ve seen in other professional yoga pants I own. But it’s still fine enough that the texture isn’t all that noticeable. I like the shape of these pants overall, BUT they tend to sag after just the first few minutes of wearing them, so I have to hike them up a little every so often. (Maybe if I had gotten a smaller size that would be less of an issue, but they aren't loose anywhere, so I was hesitant to size down.) -I’m 5’5”, and the 31” inseam falls below my ankle but doesn’t drag the floor. -It’s fairly high-waisted (comes to half an inch under my belly button). -I’m 142lbs, and the Large is a pretty good fit for me. Definitely would stretch for a few more pounds, and a medium might have actually worked better, but I’m fairly happy with the fit. Overall, a nice pair of work pants!

I may have overestimated how long my legs really are, but it's so nice to finally have pants that are long enough for my legs! I'm 5'9 and most regular pants are an inch too short on me, but being able to choose the inseam for these was excellent! I'd suggest measuring to be sure if you don't want to have to hem. The pants are constructed very well and are comfortable to wear. Love a stretch pant that passes as business wear!

Beautiful quality pants. They fit exactly as described :-) I'm always a little wary of ordering pants online because the sizing can be way off. However, these fit exactly as I expected based on the product description and sizing chart. I'm 5'4" and weigh 125lb so I typically wear an XS or size 2 in pants. However, the sizing chart on Amazon indicated a waist and hip measurements in an XS that made me think they would be too small. So, I ordered the small and they fit me perfectly. I have short legs so I knew that a 29" inseam would be too long and that I would need to hem them. However, because of the excellent product description, I was not disappointed when the product arrived and it was exactly how I expected it to be. The actual pants themselves are beautifully soft and comfortable. The material is great quality and they are extremely well made. They have quite a bit of stretch to them and so are not uncomfortable when you sit down. They have faux pockets in the back which are cute and there are real pockets in the front. I don't actually like that you can see the outline of the pocket at the front so I'm going to cut the pockets off and stitch up the pocket opening to turn them into faux pockets like at the back. These are really lovely pants and I highly recommend them!

Nice, but very see through. This pants are stretchy and comfortable. The elastic waist is also comfortable and not too tight. They look very fashionable. However, they're very see through and short. As cash be seen in the pictures, the little tag on the side is clearly visible. I'm 5'6" and they were just long enough to reach my ankles. I would have loved a longer fit.

Thin and itchy I am wearing a size small and I am 5’3, 140 lbs and my lower measurements are 27-36. The fit was fine and the material had enough stretch to move around in. But I did not like how thin and scratchy the material was, it really irritated my skin and they were really short (and I’m short). So these def aren’t for taller girls. I wasn’t a huge fan of them. They would be ok for wearing around the house.

Flowy and comfortable pants First, and most importantly, they fit me really well around the waist. These are the first pants I've owned since having a baby that I can say that about, but these are able to somehow hold onto my belly and not fall down or dig in. I think maybe it's the stretchy material and wide the ruched waistband. I'm 5'10" so finding pants long enough for me is a challenge. These are good because they are cropped and I don't think there's a particular length they have to reach like regular pants. That said, these are longer than I expected. They have not made me feel like I'm too tall to wear them. Second, and almost as important as fitting well, they are comfortable. Like mega comfortable, because they are super soft and lightweight and stretchy. If you are looking for structured pants, look elsewhere. If you want pants that you can get down in the floor to play with your kids, wear all day painlessly, and not bother to change into pajamas at bedtime because they are just that comfortable, these are for you. Third, how they look. I personally don't know what to do with these style wise. I don't own any short or cropped shirts (I'm tall haha). I think they would look cute if the waistband was more visible, but I can't style it that way without updating my wardrobe. So for the looks of the pants, I've knocked off a star simply because I don't know what to wear with them that would be a cute outfit. Fourth, the functionality of these pants surprised me. They went through the wash well with the settings I use for all my clothes (cold/cold wash, extra low dry for 1 hour, spin without heat for a while to cool). The fit didn't seem to change after washing (I have only washed once so far). The pockets are generous in size, my (fairly large) phone fits all the way inside with plenty of extra room at the top to keep it from falling out when I sit. I can even do things like sit on the floor and not only does the phone stay securely in my pocket the whole time, even when getting up, it is comfortable and I don't feel the phone jabbing my hip or anything when I move or sit. Also, the pants stay out on my waist even when adding the weight of the phone to the pocket. Over time, the waistband may get slightly crooked from the uneven load, but it doesn't make the pants fall down or hang cattywompus. I haven't found any lounge pants or even jeans that perform so well at comfortably and securely holding my phone in a pocket. Usually they do well at one (if that!) but not both. I am a SAHM and these pants work really well for my lifestyle. They are very casual, so I don't know if I feel comfortable styling them for wearing on outings. I've primarily used them at home. If you like this wide leg flowy style, for sure give them a try.

So comfy So comfortable!! I’m 5’5” and very pear shaped and I think that medium fits great. It’s still hot where I live, so it’s nice to have clothes that are flowy and cool. These also emphasize your waist which is nice. I think that the length could be a tiny bit shorter but maybe they are shorter on someone with longer legs. These would make great period or bloat pants. I’m going to wear these all the time.
Pants are excellent, had so much compliments from my staff and my students told me that I ate my swag 😂😂 so I’m getting all colors… For reference I'm 5”2 size xsmall, weight 113

Confortable ! In the picture - Teal in size large - regular length ( 5'7-168cm / 200lbs-90kg) I needed something confortable and decent enough to wear to work after having my last baby. I bought multiple colors starting with XXL after baby to now Large. These pants are great for work or casual depending on how you accessorize. Pro: - the pants are slim but not so Skinny - perfect for me as I have "big legs" - lots of stretch - Color does not fade overtime Pro or con depending on shape: - wait is slim and great for hourglass figure but has also lots of stretch Cons: - Durability, Not great! it starts to have the little elastic thread coming out of fabric after a little while and it's worst on the black with white thread version (Blackwhite-p44897sk). For the price and how the pants fits for me its acceptable. Just know that you will not keep this for years. I hope this helps. Good luck!!

Great first try on I haven’t worn these out yet, but first impressions are good. The color is rich and the material feels sturdy. I’m 5’6”, ~140 lbs and the Medium fit my waist and legs great. There is a slight bunching of the fabric near the crotch but nothing crazy. I could have gone for an inch or 2 more in length, so if you have long legs, you may be getting high waters with these. The high waist and stretchy fabric make them comfortable to sit in and pretty easy to move around in.

I was SUPER skeptical of these jeans but they are really great. I like them A LOT. I gave them 4 stars only because A) the material is such that if you have cellulite or rolls of any kind, it will probably show (the material is a little thinner) and B) because if they offered an extra small option I would probably need it but it’s not available. For reference I’m 5’5” weigh 108 lbs and my dimensions are 32-24.5-35.5. I wear a small or extra small in most clothing. I often have a hard time with pants because despite not being super tall all of my height is in my legs. So the typical 28-29 inch inseam provided by most woman’s pants is just too short. (I look for 32 inseams whenever I can find them!) But these fit great! I’d prefer they be a little bit snugger around my butt and upper thighs but all things considered these pants are a win for sure. I would say pay close attention to the photo provided by the manufacturer of the pants listing the dimensions. They seem to be pretty accurate. Seriously, give these pants a shot. Especially if you’re someone who has a hard time finding them long enough. They aren’t the longest pants I own BUT they are longer than average for sure. And they are very stretchy. I can see now why people reverence being able to fit into smaller sizes quite comfortably. I’m going to buy a few more pair in different colors :) UPDATE: Love LOVE these pants!! I got them in more colors and they all fit great. They seem to wash well too. So far the colors haven’t bled at all (though I have washed them with like colors) and they have not shrunk. I have not but them in the dryer though. I did see that some of the sizing is inconsistent but nothing significant. Also, the white pants are a bit see through so be prepared to purchase nude undies for when you wear them. Otherwise I am so super happy with this pants and I can’t stress enough how hard it is to find them that fit right (especially online). TRY THESE!!! You won’t regret it.

Love this! I got this in a S, M, and L to see which one fit the best! So I got this in a small, medium and large to see which size would fit me best. I am 5'5 for height and 115-120lbs(I haven't weighed myself recently to know my weight exactly). Waist is 29inches, hips is 34 inches. The size small was soo small! Size medium seemed too small because the zipper seemed too far out but because these pants are soo stretchy, it does zip up without feeling like the button is going to rip off. The size large for me was too loose feeling for my legs. The butt, it just didn't hug my body right. The size medium was the best choice for me. And let me tell you, it is soooo comfortable! My current new favorite pants for work especially! But... these are really nice pants for wearing while I am off of work too!I love that it is stretchy. I love that I can choose the length of these pants. I ordered the medium long. I love that there is a zipper too! Zipper plus stretchy and not thin quality is just super excellent!! Get this and find the right size for you!Edit: I accidentally put this in the dryer once! Oh no!!!! But actually, I still wear this for work. I am soo surprised that I can still wear it. The zipper part on the top portion of the pants remain still stretchy. My legs fit fine as well. The only difference that I could tell is the length of the pants. It did get a lil short after being in the dryer. Not high waters short, just a lil short. Still wearable for work for me. Since this product is stretchy, it does stretch back out some. I do not recommend putting these pants in the dryer though- it smelled a lil burnt.. hang dry is best.

Great Quality I needed these last minute for a themed dance and my daughter is between sizes as she is too tall for girls sizes without them being too large in other areas and too short for a lot of women’s and junior sizes. Her normal size is 00 or 0, she is 5’1” and 80 pounds. These are like really soft stretchy jeans, she loves the way they feel and although they are a little loose in some areas, they fit perfectly in the waist with room to grow and the length is perfect. I did wash and dry them before wearing and they did not shrink at all, nor did the color fade. Very nice pants, I would recommend!

Seriously, my New FAVORITE pair of jeans I am 5'3 and about 110 lbs, I purchased a size Medium: my legs are long and I guess for a short person my thighs and calves are bigger than the normal 5'3 person.... I'm just un proportionate... But these jeans are long enough to not look sloppy, look super cute with booties and not short enough to look like flood water pants (like the rest of my jeans). They don't ( or at lest not yet) get loose around the knees, and they have pockets! FRONT AND BACK POCKETS! which is nice b/c then my bootie doesn't look as flat as it is, and a front pocket is nice to have. I love these jeans in the fact that they are causal enough to wear to work/church and casual enough to wear to my daughters soccer practice without looking like I'm wearing leggings. I live in these jeans! Usually when i find a pair of pants, they will fit my waist and be too short or too long, or they fit my legs, but not my waist, or they are long enough and fit my waist but are too tight on my thighs... (un proportionate) These jeans are super stretchy and so comfortable that when I get home I am not dying to get out of them. They hug and fit in all the right places. I only gave 4 stars b/c the zipper doesn't like to stay up. Other than that, I want every color, I purchased black, wine and charcoal and cant decided which color is my favorite.

Amazing jeans but a little short I have been looking for colored jeans for work that are comfortable and stretchy. I'm currently in ED recovery so I'm slowly gaining weight and my sizes are all over. I LOVE that these are similar to real jeans instead of legging material but have a lot of stretch to them like leggings do. The color is great as pictured! The only downside is that they are short in length. I am 5'3, 240ish pounds and ordered a 1x. They stop right above my ankle which is a lot shorter than I normally pick but I rolled them into capris and I'm sure they will look great with fall boots! Another downside is that they are thin. I have a tattoo on my butt that if I am not wearing covering underwear can be seen a little through the material. But that's not enough to keep me from loving them! Definitely buying more colors!

Pretty great, maybe size up I really like these pants and man are they stretchy. I’m 5’9’ and about 150 lbs. (I’m usually an 8/10 in pants) I originally bought a large, but since I’m pear-shaped (W: 27.5”, H: 42.5”) they were tighter in the legs than I was wanting, although the waist fit perfectly. So I ordered an x-large and that was overall a better fit. I have to wear a belt to avoid slippage in the waist (story of my life) but the legs are not quite as a snug. (Except the calves…they are still really tight. Maybe I have big calves?) So I guess my advise would be to order a size or two up if you don’t want your pants to feel/look painted on. They seem pretty good quality. The material is a bit strange, but the color is a beautiful maroon. I’m pretty sure I’ll order another color sometime soon.

Finally found some pants for ME! Ever since I had my second baby, none of my pants fit. NONE. So I have been living in leggings and yoga pants. I was 145lbs (size 8) before and now I am 170 (size 10), so my hips got wider. I really needed Long pants, because I'm tall. These are comfy, stretchy but don't look like leggings, and the material is string enough for support and not to show cellulite (you know...) Only down side is they are TOO HIGH in the front going past my belly button which I haven't worn for decades. I'll probably order more of these pants in a size Medium when I lose the baby weight.

I want a pair in every color. Let me start off by saying I hate buying pants. Doesn't matter if it is online or in the store. I can try on 20 pairs of pants in a store and maybe, MAYBE walk out of the store with 1 pair of pants. I have a high waist, little to no eh-hem rear, and thicker thighs. This can make it very difficult to find pants that I love and that fit well. To make matters worse I am a size 7 or 27 which I have found is an increasingly hard pants size to find when you are a frugal shopper. For reference I am 5' 4" and 133 lbs. I came across these and decided to give the size Medium a shot. These pants are all I've ever wanted in pants. So far I have bought two colors: Dusty Rose and Burgundy. The colors are gorgeous. The material feels similar to jeans, but it is much softer. They are super stretchy and they fit like a glove. I really feel beautiful in them. They can be dressed up with a nice work blouse for the office or dressed down with a band t-shirt for a concert. I want to own a pair in every color and toss all my poor fitting pants. I just hope these stay available for long enough for me to buy them when I have funds. Edit: By the way... They have pockets. Real pockets. Deep pockets! Women's pants never have pockets. These are the perfect pants.

Great pants, size is forgiving, order your true size. I was looking for some comfy pants to wear during spring and summer. I’m in love with these pants, like the rest of the reviewers. I’m two months post partum, 5’9 and 220lbs. While the reviews said there was lots of stretch, I was hesitant to order my true size (XL). I ordered a 2x and it was too big. I can take them off without unbuttoning the pants! I ordered a white in 1x and fushia in xl. The xl definitely fits and is super comfortable, but as the pictures show, there is more bunching in the legs so I think the 1xl fits a little better out of the dried but the xl is better at the end of the day because it doesn’t stretch out as much. I washed the pants according to the directions and I did not notice shrinking. My pink pants did not bleed either. The white pants are see through so wear a long shirt and light underwear. Can’t go wrong with this price or these colors!

Medium WAY too big, weird cut I’m not sure what it is with the brand Hybrid & Company, but you could order five pairs of pants the same size and they’d all come out different. I always give it a 30% chance when I order…I got this red color of skinny jeans in a size small at first, and they were a bit tight and I couldn’t even button them, so I went up to a size medium which are the pants in the picture (smudge on mirror, not on pants). These must’ve been mislabeled or something because they’re HUGE. I’m 5’4” and 108 pounds but…come on. Then the smalls should’ve fit? And the cut is long & slouchy. Such a joke.

I literally love everything about these pant. They are pretty stretchy they actually stay at my ankle even after washing and drying multiple times now and still haven't shrunk up. They are actually still black and don't fade color after one wash, even now still look like when I got them and i have washed them a handful of times. There are front and back pockets!! (Which is really rare in women's pants like these) and for the price they are just AMAZING!!! I have been looking like something like these for soooo long for work because I have to wear black pants but they always tend fade out really quick or shrink up. I will definitely be getting some more! I'm 5'5 and between 103lb-107lb and wear a size 2 in all pants I own

Super stretch a tad long for petite women Purchased black in size medium for my daughter. She is 5’3” and about 140lbs. She likes the fit as they are skinny jegging type of pants, with super stretch. She wasn’t thrilled with them not being high waisted pants. She usually wears a size 8 short in AE jeans for comparison. Will update for washing and durability. Overall I think they’re perfect to wear for work or even dress up with a cute sweater or shirt. Update: washed alone and hung up to dry. I then decided to throw into dryer for five minutes to maybe shrink a little. They came out fine, and I’m ordering a second pair.
